name: Charles Oliver Miller
description: While at the University of Georgia, Tootsie has always impressed the people with whom he associated with his friendliness, his good humor, his generosity and unselfishness. To those who knew him, his pleasant laugh was ever agreeable, ever attractive. Charming and unspoiled, always ready to play, he never outgrew the irresponsible days of his freshman year. Even to this day he takes part in hazing freshmen with all the zest of the lustiest sophomore. Tootsie's effervescent laugh, his sheer good nature, have created, if not always hordes of prostate admirers, at least a favorable permanent impression. To have played with him and listened to his sparkling wit is a pleasure; to have seen the lovable nature under his buffoonry is charming; and to have known his friendship for four years is a rare privilege and a delight not lightly to be forgotten. May Fortune's smile be as affable as your own.

name: James Zenus Miller
motto: "A good line o' Bull, backed by information, goes a long way."
clubs: First Lieutenant R. O. T. C.
description: "Shorty" is one of those steady, quiet, unassuming fellows who can always be counted on as a loyal friend. He is of the kind that gradually work their way into the hearts of their fellow men. Not only has he proven himself a desirable associate but he is without a doubt a man who stands ready to lend a helping hand to whomever might call upon him. Besides being popular with his male friends, "J. Z." is very much in favor with the opposite sex. His industry and determination have been the chief factors in his very successful and progressive college career during the four years that he has been at the University. He is not an honor man in his studies but he has shown that he has known how to do good work consistently and all of his friends feel safe when they predict for him success in whatever he undertakes.

name: William Arthur Minor, Jr.
motto: "My usefulness in this world is measured by the good I can do others."
description: Arthur Minor is a substantial representative of Stone Mountain. Majoring in the serious pursuit of an education, he has not neglected to minor in the just as useful occupation of learning to know people. His four years of college have been full of consistent studying, self-supporting work, and friendship-making. With a winning personality he has acquired a few real friends and in addition a wide circle of pleasant acquaintances. Arthur's striking characteristic is his pure and unalloyed unselfishness. His considerate regard for others is remarkable because it is so whole-souled and perfectly genuine. The future holds much for Minor. His high scholastic attainments, his knowledge of human nature, his initiative and energy, his habit of tackling every task with vim, are sure to land him at the top.

name: Mrs. Leila Ritchie Mize
motto: "I'll not budge an inch."
description: "Sister Leila" came "off the road" from her job of pushing her twenty-four counties onto the band wagon of progress, as a District Agent in the Agricultural College Extension Service, and in one year's work together with her credits from the G. S. W. C. (and various short courses here and elsewhere) she has not only obtained her diploma but has graduated with honors, in spite of the fact that her motto is, "Never do today what you can put off till tomorrow." The outstanding feature of her Georgia career is the fact that she shot both "A plus Dixon" and Prof. Tuttle for 100 on examination. Another evidence of her brilliancy is that when called on for a report which she had not prepared she made such a noble and agile effort that Prof. Proctor did not "compre" the situation, but passed her up for "perfect". We wish her happiness and success in life.

We wish her happiness and success in life.

name: Clarence Abna Moody, D.V.M.
motto: "Let me live in a house by the side of the road and be a friend to man."
description: "Dugan," as he is familiarly known, entered the University to study the gentle art of rolling pills, but after a year of that he decided that his nature craved more action. Accordingly, he took up Veterinary Medicine as a study. Moody, by virtue of the fact that he has attended three summer schools, is finishing his course, a regular four year course and requiring more hours for graduation than any other course in college, in three years. Despite the fact that he is red-headed, "Dugan" has a good disposition and a friendliness which has made him numerous friends—not only among the boys but among the Co-eds as well. He has been a consistent, faithful and hardworking student and should do well in his life's work. His ability to remember the action of certain drugs has been phenomenal. By working hard and always keeping in mind that "the bull is mightier than the bullet," you will surely succeed. The best of luck to you, "Dugan".

name: George Walter Murphey, B. S. Ag.
motto: "Ambitions are necessary, even if the majority are infertile, and never hatch."
description: George is a man of sterling character and intellectual independence. Just an upward glance at his stern determined countenance will readily convince one that George is a man that cannot be denied the reward of perseverance. He is a product of the city of Wadley, and on his brief stay here he has endeavored to make the little Metropolis famous from the standpoint of Social Endeavor and Chickenology. As a military man he is known far and wide, for in the early morning his reverberating commands can be heard as he commands the "Invisible Squadron" on the fields of service. George is an authority on chickens (both kinds) and being a mean "Bull Slinger" he expects to make money in his home county by establishing an information bureau which will run on the Piggly Wiggly basis. Luck to you George.

name: Lois Blonnie Musgrove, B.S.C.
description: Mary should indeed be proud of his record at Georgia. He has been a vital part, at some time or other, in every phase of campus life. On several trips of the teams of the Red and Black, he has been present. Always at the stands, Manager Musgrove would greet you with a smile. In the classroom he commanded the love and respect of both students and professor. At social gatherings he was always numbered among those present. An irresistible personality has won for him a host of friends. The fairer sex are inclined to bestow favors and affections upon him; and in many ways and at various times, Lady Fortune has smiled upon him. You cannot keep a good man down, and Lois Blonnie Musgrove is headed for a star.

name: John Brown McCarty, B.S.C.
motto: "Thou art e'en as just a man as ere my conversation coped withal."
description: Possessing the old nom de plume by which so many men of great wealth and position are known, and realizing the mistake he had made in attending the would-be muchly renowned technical institution by which Atlanta aspires to make Georgia famous, "Mack" joined the fold in nineteen twenty-one, a sophomore in credits but a freshman in experience. In his hot pursuit of commercial knowledge he has managed to grab the tail. With that inalienable tenacity, characteristic of all "Macks", and his fleetness of foot being increased by a few years of practical experience in the business world, we are confident that he will overtake and subdue that creature to his own advancement. "Mack" has a very friendly disposition and is well liked by everybody. He has made a good record while here and we expect to hear great things of him some day.

name: Luther Claude McCommons, Jr.
motto: "Knowledge is of more value than riches."
description: From the little town of Greensboro, "Mac" took up his stakes several years ago and set out for the "Classic City"—to become a student at Georgia. "How're you gettin' along with your work" is his favorite query, but you needn't repeat the question to him, for it's five to one that he just shot the Professor up for a ten. "Nip" is the kind of man who is always smiling and has a never-failing good humor which has endeared him to us. Since his Freshman year, no matter how dreary the outlook, he has always brought sunshine with him. It is to be regretted that "Nip" has never had any love affairs. "Captain Billy" says, "The longer they wait, the harder they fall." If this is true, "Mac" is certainly due for a terrible bump. As a good sport and true friend, the kind who would lend you his last cent, you'd look a long time before finding another "Mac". May life hold many pleasant things for you, as your affable nature deserves.

name: Charles Cicero McCutcheon
motto: "What's worth doing at all is worth doing well."
description: In search of new fields to conquer, Charlie came to us this fall from Dahlonega. Truly, he has conquered our hearts by the friendliness of his personality, and won our admiration through his sincere and steadfast loyalty to purpose. We cannot know what the coming years will yield, but we hope, Charlie, as we follow the marks of your progress in the sands of time, to some day find your name burned on the wall of the Hall of Fame. Although you are a new comer, you have truly become one of us. We have been honored to welcome you; we have been proud to claim you as a classmate and as a friend; and, as you sally forth on the great adventure, "Life," armed with the gift of our Alma Mater, wherever you may go, and whatever you may do, you bear the best wishes of the class of '24.

name: John Howard McGehee, Jr.
motto: "Because right is right, to fight for right were wisdom in the scorn of consequence."
clubs: Business Manager of Red and Black
description: With the graduation of "Uncle John" our University loses, to say the least, one of her most valuable sons. During the four years that he has been here, he has been an active participant in many college activities—has distinguished himself in the classroom and out. The honors written beneath his name bear testimony to the achievements and successes which are his. These things demand the respect and admiration of the world, but we, whose privilege it has been to live in close association with him for four years, have come to love him for himself. His personality and strength of character have won for him friends wherever he has gone.

name: George Remur McKinnon
motto: "What I aspired to be and was not comforts me."
description: We present for your approval George McKinnon, who hails from the town of Boston, but assumes no responsibility for the famous Baked Beans and the Tea Party controversy. He appeared on the campus in the fall of 1921, right out of the Southwest Georgia banking circles. He has made a good record in the class room and has been a favorite among his many associates. Our hero has not confined his endeavors to the campus alone, but has attained quite a lot of notoriety as well as a remarkable reputation with the Athens Police Force through outside activities. Mc has donated liberally to all the campus welfare societies and has also contributed bounteously to the many punch boards that have floated into his path. George, for that is his cognomen among the Co-eds, is noted for his never-missing attendance at the Colonial and his untiring devotion to the Strand. We predict for Mc a life full of success.

name: Walter Floy McLendon, D.V.M.
motto: "Be a whole man for one thing at a time."
description: This gentleman is none other than our friend "Fat" who believes in standing behind the gun and in front of a mule. He began his college career by turning in a false fire alarm and has kept up the false alarm part of it for four years. Many of his sayings are axioms. He demonstrated that a "Good run is better than a bad stand," by departing from the beaten path of Lumpkin Street for the safety that was afforded by that portion of his boarding house which lay between the floor and the ground. Fat is a very fastidious person for he has been known to travel eleven miles for a particular brand of hair-cut, and even with opportunity within his grasp he has been known to suddenly change his mind and decline that for which his soul had yearned. Because of lack of space his most harrowing experiences cannot be related here.

name: Joseph Barnwell Newton, B.S.A.
motto: "The myriads of generations of the future are of more importance than our one of today."
description: The handsome "geek" you see portrayed above in all his glory is none other than our old delightful, jolly, reliable Puss. As a joy spreader and a joke puller, Puss has taken the rag these last four years. He tells great stories about his ability to pitch wheat, and his line has got the majority of college boys thinking he is a famous character—of course there is no doubt of this. Puss is a swamp alligator from the little town of Woodbine, Georgia. He entered the Eleventh District A. and M., and made a great record at that worthy institution. As an athlete Puss demonstrated illimitable possibilities, but due to an inherited tendency to accumulate avoirdupois, and an opulent training table, he never did quite reach his best form. He very much regrets that he was not born fifty years earlier, when the colossal, gigantic, beefy steam roller type held undisputed sway, without the semblance of a challenge over the gridirons of history.

name: Donald Fraser Orr, A.B.
motto: "Why care for fame, if you do a few things well, or one the best you can."
description: Not many men who have done as much as Donald has in his four-year sojourn here have made so little fuss about it. He has gone about his work in a quiet and unassuming way; but there are few courses of a broad cultural value that are not on his credit card with a good grade attached. He is not a "jolly good fellow"—a man that gives his hand to anyone and his heart to none. He is, however, as true and lovable a friend as one could want. In Donald you find a splendid example of the type of man that inherently shuns anything false and insincere. His ideals are high and he has the manhood to attain them—we regret that there are so few like him. In this age, it seems that few strive to be true to themselves. Happily, Donald is an exception.

name: J. W. Overstreet
motto: "Labor, laughter, and love."
description: Bill came to us from Annapolis, where he completed three years of diligent study and he is now claimed by Georgia as one of her sons. A goodly amount of sunshine that Bill has in his personal equipment has won for him a place in the hearts of all who know him, and they are many; both male and female. In social affairs he is the idol of his friends. In his studies he is one of the most brilliant men in college. A man devoted to his study and sincere in his purpose. We predict for him no less achievement than his ambition demands. Bill is quite a busy man but nevertheless he always finds time to slip off to Lucy Cobb. There certainly must be some attraction out there for Bill for he has been out to all the receptions; he is rather strong for the fair sex. Truly, everyone who knows him can best say, "this is the man after mine own heart."

name: Cliff Pantone
motto: "Measure not success in money but in friends."
description: "He came; he saw; he conquered." Not an empire, gentle reader, but a place in the heart of every lover of sports; for "Pannie" has endeared himself to friend and foe alike. He possesses that unmistakable quality of genuine sportsmanship. Modest in victory, game in defeat, the name of Clifford Pantone deserves a niche in the Hall of Fame at the University and truly has he earned it by his untiring and ceaseless efforts to give his all for his Alma Mater. "The noblest Roman of them all." All-Southern his freshman year, Pannie performed even more brilliantly his second year, and paved the way for Georgia's first Southern Championship in many years. The same determination so characteristic of him on the diamond, applied in the business world, can bring only huge success for one who has been faithful to his trust, weighed and found worthy.

name: John Leslie Parker
motto: "The only way to have friends is to be one."
description: Try to be calm while you look upon the countenance of this young gentleman from Whigham, Georgia. During the four years we have known "High Pocket" we have tried to locate his town but all in vain. From all reports it is a town of the fair sex, and his popularity there is no less than around a certain place in Athens, which he frequents to the delight of the ladies therein. Nevertheless, he states that he is immune from the charms of women. Being handsome is one of the least of his merits. Under the tutelage of "Little Charlie" he has acquired many of the fundamentals of Engineering, Civil and otherwise, and we are sure that wherever he goes from here he will be successful. He is a great talker among his friends, but calm and reserved around strangers. He is a congenial and dependable lad, and an all round good fellow. Our best wishes go with you Parker.

name: Bessie Parr
motto: "Neither to laugh nor to cry, but to understand."
enrolltime: During her two years' stay at the University
description: At a glance, one would hardly think that this demure young maid was equally proficient in the culinary art and in guiding the destinies of the Student Government of Soule Hall over a smooth year of progress; but so she is. During her two years' stay at the University, Bessie has been seen spending quite a bit of her time around the genetics department of LeConte Hall, but we have not noticed that she has begun the process of evolution yet. In fact, one would feel quite safe in saying that no matter what degree of success she may attain, whether it be in the Thalian Play or later in the broader walks of life, that she will remain unspoiled by it; and that she will meet success and adversity alike with the same quaint little smile. We wish her success and hope that every happiness will be hers.

name: Harold Telford Patterson
motto: "Length without breadth is not good measure."
enrolltime: During his four years in college
description: After finishing Athens High School, Harold decided to finish his learning by entering his State University. During his four years in college, besides winning countless numbers of friends, he has shown an unusual combination of ability and hard work. His ability to lead men is shown by his marked success in the R. O. T. C., where he holds the rank of captain. Always conscientious, and at the same time cordial and friendly, he holds the respect of everyone he comes in contact with, from the lowliest freshman to the most learned senior. As he leaves here to delve further into the field of knowledge he takes with him a B. S. degree and an A. M. almost finished as a symbol of his seriousness of purpose. May you have the best fortune in all your undertakings, and may success and your lady love smile sweetly on you. "Q. E. D."

name: William Kuhlke Philpot, B.S.
description: The more or less handsome features here reproduced belong to none other than William Kuhlke Philpot. A long name for such a short and simple fellow. Well, we think Billie does suit him better. "Billie's" check comes from Augusta, his prep "dip" came from A. R. C., and his special deliveries come from all parts of the country. In spite of his size, Billie's pluck and perseverance won for him a football G this year. It might be mentioned that he is graduating in three years, and that, in spite of his many activities outside the classroom, he has made some very creditable marks. All joking aside, folks, Billie has made a great many friends and a great record at Georgia. We believe that he is destined to do big things in this little old world.

name: Francis Dowdle Pierce, B.S.
motto: "Knowledge is proud that he has learned so much; wisdom is humble that he knows no more."
description: "Doodle Bug," though small in stature, fills a big place in the hearts of his friends. Through his smiles and pleasing manners he has won many friends. "Bug," though not an intellectual genius, has stood well in his classes, and his professors respect him as a man who has done his duty. "Doodle's" place will be hard to fill, for a finer, nobler gentleman has never entered life from the doors of the University. As an actor, he has made many beautiful girls wish they were the heroine in the many love scenes he has entered into on the stage, as well as otherwise. When Parrott sent this young man forth, it settled beyond a doubt the question whether this thriving town should ever be on the map. Whether as a surgeon who performs miracles with his instruments, or as an actor whose histrionic ability astonishes the world, Doodle is fated to make his mark.

name: James Andrew Power
motto: "He who respects himself will earn the respect of all the world."
description: Pat has for three years caught about the most consistent brand of baseball ever seen here on Sanford Field. In fact the position behind the bat has been so well taken care of that the fans have quit worrying about it. Pat does not excel alone in baseball. He has faithfully attended to his studies, and it can truthfully be said he has as many friends as any man in school. In future years when the afternoons begin to get warm in Spring and the crack of bat and ball is heard on Sanford Field, Georgia will miss Power in the catcher's box. No matter how those in the stands may feel about it, his services will still not be lost to his college. We are sure he will then be reflecting credit on the Red and Black in whatever he chooses for his life work.

name: Amy Quillian
description: Amy is the kind of girl to whom flattery is an enigma. There is nothing of the veneer, or tinsel about her. Her ideals are of the highest nature, and she has strength of character to live up to them, with an accompanying breadth of mind that enables her to "get the other fellow's viewpoint" She is emblematic of all that is best and highest in womanhood. True to her convictions; true to her friends; just when a judgment is to be made; and dependable when needed. She is a rare combination. We are proud of her as a classmate and as a friend, and the University will always be glad to claim her as its own. We cannot forget you, Amy, but will often wish for your happy, contagious smile. And though the tide of circumstance force us to opposite ends of the earth, memory of you will be cherished in our hearts as an ideal. We wish you every happiness and success in life.

name: Albert Rauzin, B.S.C.
motto: "Some care for the glories of this world; and some sigh for the Prophet's Paradise to come; Ah, take the Cash and let the Credit go, nor heed the rumble of the distant Drum."
description: Pause a moment in your perambulations through this volume and gaze with reverent awe at the image of the one who is looking at you from the top of the page. Named after the king of tobaccolan his person resembles the caricature of the famous beef trust. But praise the fates that he is also possessed with an abundant supply of brains as beef. The gods never in their whole history were more satisfied with any creature of their handicraft than the specimen shown above. The sun has never set on such a remarkable individual in all of its travelling through the heavenly course. When the shades of night have fallen and the sun has gone to rest for the night, the moon comes out and looks down upon this person and bends its head and cries for shame.

name: Clarence Moses Reed, B.S.A.
high school: Tenth District A. and M. School
motto: "The respect you have for yourself will determine the respect others have for you."
enrolltime: entered the University four years ago
description: "C. M." as he is known by his College chums, hails from Lincolnton. He entered the University four years ago to continue his studies in the field of Agriculture, after having graduated from the Tenth District A. and M. School. Besides being a ladies' man, Clarence has a number of male associates. His smile and warm handshake have won for him a host of friends. In class work, Clarence always stood well. He is a good worker. No task ever seems to hard for him to tackle. By his efforts, he has made a record that anyone should be proud to claim. As a further evidence of his ability we credit him with the honor of having defrayed his college expenses by his own initiative. With this in mind, we prophesy for him great success. Luck to you, Reed.

name: Millard Arnold Reeves, B.S.C.
motto: "You are master of the unspoken word but the spoken word is master of you."
description: "Teddy's" philosophy of life is peculiarly appropriate, for it is characteristic of him, that when he thinks a thing is right, he'll say so, and not before. During his eventful sojourn here, he has done full justice to his work, but has not been unmindful of the fact that a taking personality is one of man's most valuable assets. This view of life has won for him a host of friends. While not endowed with a talent and physique so necessary to athletic prowess, he has striven unceasingly for the benefit of the varsity teams and was amply rewarded in his Senior year by being made student manager. It may be said of Reeves that he is persistent without being obstinate. This admirable quality will assure his success in the future.

name: Lucien Emerson Roberts, A.B.
motto: "An ounce of 'Bull' is of more value than a pound of 'Bone'."
description: Lucien Emerson Roberts is from Dallas, Georgia. For the last three years he has worked conscientiously and now will be rewarded with his A. B. degree. Lucien is well known among the representatives of the female contingent of the University. Lately he has grown a mustache and has learned to "trip the light fantastic toe," much to the delight of the ladies. Aside from these virtues our hero has many others but space forbids us to mention them. Before coming to Georgia, Roberts was professor at Sweet Water Creek Academy, in Paulding County. We can safely predict a great future for him in this profession. There is some chance, however, that he may change his mind, so don't be surprised if some day we hear of him fighting for "mountain dew" in Congress. But be that as it may, we feel sure that Roberts will do well in any line of work he may take up. We send him off with our best wishes.

name: Walter Leonard Robinson, B.S.C.E.
motto: "Ever within the strife of your own thoughts, obey the nobler impulse."
description: He has a sweetheart in every port. The inmates of the ladies' institution at Milledgeville hopefully await his arrival, per Ford, ever so often. When he strolls nonchalantly out Milledge Avenue, the whole and entire student body, including " . . . . . ." of Lucy Cobb, appear en masse to gaze upon their prince charming. To the heretofore common belief that no man can be a "sheik" and a scholar, simultaneously, we here take issue, and cite a living example, in the person of this man. He leaves with an enviable scholastic record. When America's greatest engineers are listed in the proverbial "Who's Who," the name of Walter Leonard Robinson will be among those present. To know him is to love him, to have his friendship is an asset, and to lose him is to suffer an irremediable loss. More power to you, "Robbie."

name: William Spencer Rockwell, B.S.A.
motto: "Be not shaken from thy purpose, either by the plaudits of the mob, or the abuse of the envious."
clubs: First Lieutenant M. T. C. Corps
description: An understanding study of Spence will reveal a character which is the physical embodiment of his motto. Steadfast and loyal, tried and true, he has made many friends and no few feminine admirers in his four years stay at Georgia. Honors have come his way but he has not sought them; they are rather the result of his earnest endeavor which will ever be recognized and admired by those who know him. He is possessed of a character rare as a jewel, with only one black spot upon it, which is a certain episode occurring in a coal house. Spence has not climbed the Ag hill four years in vain. He is destined to make a mark in this world, and to continue to hold a large place in the hearts of his friends. For one of such steadfast character, the world holds many rewards.

name: Frederick Lorenzo Russell, Jr.
motto: "Make every failure a stepping stone to success."
description: The usual recommendation to the world must needs be revised to fit this rare variety of Homo Sapiens, for Fred is most decidedly untypical and individualistic. His quiet, unassuming manner and his very admirable habit of never flaunting to the world his many virtues has placed him high in the esteem of all those whose good fortune it is to know him. His inborn consideration of the pleasure of others and indeed his every manner bespeaks him as a true gentleman. His nature runs rather to the profound, we are prone to think, for he is ever found prying into those abstract subjects which go by little observed or entirely unseen by the average college student. Surely this inquiring nature with the innate perception which is his will lead him to fame some day. Fred, you are a man worth knowing, but most of all, you are a MAN. The best wishes of all go with you.

name: Fenley Ryther
description: "Ted" has seen most of the lands where the American flag flies but Georgia is his choice of them all. Four years ago Col. Ryther was ordered to the University, and "Ted" enrolled as a student. No native born son has ever been a more loyal supporter of Georgia; for loyalty is the keynote of his character; loyalty to his school and to his friends. His sincere good fellowship has won him a host of friends on the campus, in Athens, and last but not least at the Co-ed Barn. Social activities, however, have occupied but a small part of his attention as his high standing in the Engineering department shows. Computing the interchangeability of the center of percussion and the instantaneous axis is mere child's play for Ted. Should war come, Lieutenant Fenley Ryther will be ready for the battle.

name: Marion Dwight Sanders
motto: "It matters not if one is born in a duck yard, if one is hatched from a swan's egg."
description: To those who know him, nothing need be said; but those who have not had the pleasure of becoming intimately acquainted with him could learn much from observing this determined and steady worker. He has shown great ability and has found time to demonstrate his versatility in many ways. He is the kind to practice, not preach his convictions. Sincere, unrelenting in purpose, and successful in attaining his ambitions, he has not allowed the higher and finer things of life to be neglected. If he has been your close friend, you may well consider yourself a fortunate person; for, once you become that, you may rest content in the realization that he will be true to your confidence. He has done what was expected of him, and as he goes out on Life's Highway, he carries the good wishes of a host of friends.

name: Homer Reynolds Sanford
motto: "Let me live in a house by the side of the road and be a friend of man."
description: Young Sanford entered Georgia in the fall of 1921, and he is graduating with a degree in Commerce in three years. Some stepping. It is rumored that he will eventually enter the ministry so it is obvious why he took more or less of a business course. "Cutie" did his High schooling at Athens High, where he smashed feminine hearts galore. He's a woman hater now, though, and hasn't had a girl in many years. "Cutie" has won many honors since he has been at Georgia, and has made countless friends on the campus. He is very versatile, being a diligent student, a close observer, an athletic manager and a social lion when he wants to "do his stuff." He will always have our best wishes in his battles against old man J. John Life.

name: Ralph Benjamin Shaw, B.S.C.E.
motto: "It isn't so much what we make, but how it is made."
description: From Locust Grove Institute in the fall of 1920 there appeared on the campus a young man aspiring to become a civil engineer. He tackled the job in his old Irish fashion, and has stuck with it through exams and other periods of depression. This young man also aspired to become a soldier as well as an engineer and is well known for his success in the cavalry unit where he has won the title of being one of the best horsemen and leaders in the cavalry. Now, kind readers, before continuing, allow me to present to you the subject of my many words—Ralph Benjamin Shaw of Woodville, Georgia, better known to all of us as "Red." His record speaks for him and although he may soon be gone from among us his deeds and influence will remain with us for years to come.

name: Cyrus Newton Shearer, B.S.C.
motto: "You can't remodel the past—the future is only yours to anticipate—but now is our time to shape as we will."
description: “Cyrus the Great.” Here is a man who has done justice to his name. He is a conqueror in every way. Forsaking school after graduating from one of our most prominent preparatory schools, he decided to go into business. He soon saw, however, that he had made a great mistake and so came to our fair city. He looked the place over and then started talking. He has kept it up and has literally conquered our learned faculty by an uncanny ability to say the right thing at the right time. An everlasting flow of the well known “bull” has carried him through every tight place,—but he has always had the stuff to back his talk up. “Cy” is essentially a doer, and we feel sure that he is going to do his part towards making this world a better place to live and work in.

name: Stonewall Jackson Shirley
motto: "'Tis wise to think what you speak, rather than at all times speak what you think."
description: Ladies and Gentlemen, this is none other than Stonewall Jackson Sandy Shirley. Unlike the great man who bore the same name, Stonewall Jackson Shirley has devoted his time to the pursuit of peace and happiness. Sandy Shirley, who possesses that sandy colored curly hair is the pride of the Veterinary Division. He qualified for the title of "Sheik" by an unusual exhibition on the sand table. For his untiring efforts to successfully storm the Co-ed Barn he has been elected Knight of the Garter. Sandy is a good student, a debater who is above the average and a good all-round fellow. He was selected by his fellow students to represent his department on the Student Council, which attests to the fact that he is held in high esteem by those who know him well.

name: John Winton Sibert, Jr.
motto: "The most wasted of all days is the day when we have not laughed."
description: May we not take a goodly share of pardonable pride in presenting Johnnie! Augusta will do well to claim him as a native son in the future, as she has done in the past, for it is rumored that this learned young engineer is going to seek a larger field to reap the harvest of his lately acquired knowledge. He has achieved the happy medium of just the right amount of work and play necessary to make a real college education. He has a University career filled with pleasant recollections and few regrets. Dame Fortune smiled upon you, "Johnnie," when she cast upon you ability and good looks, coupled with a sense of humor. Give the world the best that is in you and success is yours. We wish you happiness, full-rounded happiness, the joy that comes with achievement, and the satisfaction that comes with lofty ideals.

name: David Barrow Snelling
motto: "No endeavor is in vain. Its reward is in the doing."
description: Dave, as he is familiarly known, or sometimes Dean Jr., is a product of the Classic City, and it should be proud to call him so. He is a three year man and has done well in his studies despite a strenuous schedule. In the various college activities he has taken an active part. He has "cut quite a figure" especially on social occasions, as members of the fairer sex are very easily captivated by his good looks and easy going manner. A quiet, unassuming fellow, Dave has perhaps as few enemies as any man in college. He intends to pursue the study of Medicine at Harvard University, and we predict for him a great future in his chosen profession. Then, after breaking all the ladies' hearts in sight, we believe it is his intention to settle down to the simple life in some sequestered "love nest" where two hearts beat as one. Here's to you, Dave.

name: Carl McKinley Spann
motto: "Truth and sincerity rest equal in friendship."
clubs: President, Vice-President, Secretary, and Treasurer of his German class
description: The University is judged by the outside world in accordance with the type of men she turns out each year. What a man is after he leaves college will be attributed in some degree to the instruction, the guidance, and the general development he received from his Alma Mater. We truthfully say that we are glad to send Carl out as a representative by which his College can be judged. Spann has enjoyed the distinction of being President, Vice-President, Secretary, and Treasurer of his German class. (He is the only student in it.) He has taken a very active part in athletics ever since he entered college. In the past three seasons he has worked diligently with the track squad, and this Spring his friends are hoping to see him rewarded by winning the Varsity G. He decided to try basket-ball his last year in college and surprised all his friends by immediately making the varsity squad.

name: John W. Stephenson
motto: "Determination and Faith in God will win for any man."
description: Red came to us in the fall of 1920 with the determination to accomplish two things, namely, get his degree and make some lasting friends. He has more than succeeded in both of these ambitions. His friends here are too numerous to count, and he has done well in his work. As a ladies man, Red is "All-American" material. He is always in demand with the fair sex. As a student, Red has for four years been one of the leading men in his class. He has made a very high scholastic average, and has taken an active part in most of the other major college activities. As a politician Red has few equals, having been selected as the "Sheik" of Candler Apartments. Red stoutly denies "politicking" for this place. We predict for Red the same success in life that he has enjoyed here. Luck to you old fellow, we are pulling for you.

name: Gertrude Ernestine Stith
motto: "The reward of work is more work."
enrolltime: Since her arrival at the University
description: If we had to choose one word that would describe "Stith" without a moment's hesitation we would say "enthusiastic." Since her arrival at the University her pep and enthusiasm have found outlets in every conceivable sort of activity. It has almost reached the point where Stith has to find someone to run the University while she is taking a vacation. Her long list of honors is indicative of the high esteem in which she is held by her fellow students. Stith's sole interest in the opposite sex has manifested itself either in comradeship or Hero-worship.—Sanford Field being her Shrine. A strong character, a fun-loving disposition, and ability, combined with determination will carry Stith to her goal in life, no matter where she may seek it.

name: William Granberry Taliaferro
motto: "He who lives without folly is not so wise as he thinks."
description: "A gentleman of sterling character"—no words could be better fitted to describe Bill, and be sure to take them in their full sense. A man of ability, honor, and determination, he well deserves the laurels he has won during his four years at the University. He believes that Agricultural Engineering is the profession of the future, and after listening to his convincing discourse on the subject you will believe so too. He has the good will of a host of friends, coupled with a ready wit, real ability as a public speaker, and the knowledge that comes only with experience.

name: William Tate
motto: "Bear your defeats without bitterness."
description: If I were asked, "Who has gotten the most out of Georgia during the last four years?" I would answer without hesitation, "Bill Tate." The boy who came here four years ago is now a man, and a man in the truest sense of the word. He has taken part in every college activity and has attained marked success in them. His scholastic record is an enviable one; on the debating floor he strikes joy into the hearts of his colleagues and fear into the minds of his opponents; and his record as a long distance runner shows what intelligent training will do.

name: Webb Tatum, B.S.A.
motto: "What I think I utter, and spend my malice in my breath."
description: Stop, gentlemen, and listen, but for your own sake, don't look, for you might be frightened. But let not his countenance disturb you, for a better fellow you'll never run across. He knows no conceit, ingratitude or hypocrisy. He's just a doggone good fellow and he always meets you with a grin. Big "Web" strutted his stuff for some fifteen months overseas, and when it was all over he found himself to be the possessor of many scars and that his physical arrangement had been worked over and built on a different plan. Since the war, "Big Tatum" has spent his time in the pursuit of knowledge and he has an excellent college record to his credit. This is the kind of man upon whom the stability of a nation rests,—willing to die in time of war and willing to work in time of peace. Here's to you, "Big Boy"; may the soils of Walker serve you well, and we hope and predict for you a future glowing with success.

name: George Allen Townsend, B.S.A.
motto: "Do your best at all times,—one can never tell who is watching."
clubs: Student Instructor Department of Poultry Husbandry, '23-'24
description: Attention, fellows, while I inform those who do not know it of the record of this gentleman who hails from Kite, Georgia. Hearing the call to arms in 1917, he could not resist, and soon he was across the Pond fighting his country's battles in No Man's Land. Returning from the fields of France he made his way straight to the Ag. Hill. "George" is a leader in the Agricultural Department. For four years he has proven his ability to master the problems of college life. The calmness with which he meets his daily tasks places him among the minority whose quick mentality creates thoughts which crowd the tongue for expression. His example as a student has been an inspiration to his fellows. The highest hopes for your success shall ever be present in the hearts of your fellow-students.

name: Andrew Jackson Trawick, D.V.M.
motto: "Take what Nature gives you and be thankful for it."
clubs: Winner of One Year Scholarship in '20
President of Saddle and Sirloin Club, '23
description: Red Trawick, the man who is thankful for what nature gave him, possesses physical charms which cannot be adequately expressed by mere spoken or written words. His accomplishments are very much like those of that long, lean, lithe and strong character in the "Persian Kitty." He has the experience to hand out. Red is an honor man but not a book worm. His field of activity has been a wide one, his endeavors extending from the garnering of college honors to an active participation in the conventional lighter diversions of society. He has been victorious in every encounter with one exception. This came when he attempted to take the "Little Red Hen" into camp. His pleasing personality, which enables him to make friends and hold them, and his ability to deliver the goods in a first rate manner lead us to predict for him a most successful career.

name: Kenneth Treanor, B.S.A.
motto: "a road that leads away to prospects white and fair."
description: S-s-s-s-sh, look (Oh girls, observe that walk and the haberdashery, will you?) yes, it's Red himself—chemist, statistician, agricultural economist, embryo pedagogue, etc., etc.! Certain sophomores might have handled this lamb more gently in the shearing season three years ago had they divined that this innocent youngling would don the cap and gown and receive the sheepskin even with themselves. Red has made it in three years, with an enviable record to his credit, not only for scholarship, but for general camaraderie among his fellows in his sojourn at Georgia. Unafraid of work, cheerful always, endowed with a capacity for clear thought and research, what can we predict for you but— Red has always been popular with his classmates and leaves behind him a regretful memory, a fragrant perfume, a wailing strain of a violin.

name: Genevieve Tucker
motto: "Grasp every opportunity; heed not what others may say."
description: From the Palmetto State via the State Normal School came "Jenny V" to Georgia in '22. Her many friends at the Co-ed Barn and the Home Economics Faculty have put out the good work that "Jenny V's" calories all produce energy for other people. From bust of dawn until curfew toots at Soule Hall she may be seen dashing from one good deed to another, always helping a friend in need, and that too with a happy smile as if she really enjoys being allowed to help. With this disposition and her Georgia training, one would prognosticate with ease that she will make a noble helpmate for some enterprising South Carolinian who desires real quality in his partner for life. Whether she has ever thought of the following words or not, they express what her friends think of as having been her motto: "I'll help you whether you need it or not."

name: Jim Chandler Turner
enrolltime: During her two years' stay at the University
description: When we first learned that Jim Turner was to attend the University and stay at the "Co-ed Barn" the girls were all thrilled over the prospect of having with us a co-ed of the male sex and looked forward to seeing a dashing young fellow who would probably be a football star. Imagine the surprise over beholding a typical "female of the species" who was to take Home Economics and who excelled in all lines of domestic art. During her two years' stay at the University, she has made hosts of friends who cannot forget her frank, hearty disposition. We predict for her a successful career in her chosen profession, and if this independent young woman ever succumbs to the wiles of a designing member of the male sex, lucky will be the man. Here's to you, Jim, and may you find in life the realization of every ambition; and friends who will appreciate you as do your college mates at old Georgia.

name: Raul daSilva Vieitas
motto: "Love may not make the world go round, but it makes me go round the world."
description: Entrusted to our hospitality by our sister state of South America, Ralph came as a gentle reminder of the love we lose when we fight for selfish ends. The refinement of a high society, the culture of Europe, and a virile Latin temperament showed us a rare example of the passing days of chivalry. Refinement was the essence of his nature. Love of music, art and girls was where it found expression. Adaptation was his own private miracle. Under the estranging appellation of "foreigner" he suddenly lost his hair, mixed in with the rest, and became a Georgia fellow. First hoboing, then hazing, loafing, and then leading, he passed through the regular four years of college life simply as one of the bunch. Ralph leaves now for Brazil with the hearts of a group of close friends, the admiration of his critics, and many smiles from his "Georgia Peaches."

name: John Tracy Walker
motto: "Words without action is like smiling at a woman in the dark."
description: Tracy was with us before the war; and, after serving honorably and faithfully through that tumultuous conflict, he came back home with the same old love for his University. Most every one in school (including the ladies) has received that pleasant smile and cheery greeting of his. He is known from one end of college to the other for that trait of character. And, truth to tell, he has a friend for every smile that he has given. Tracy has all the qualifications requisite for a successful life, and with a personality as pleasing as his, and the natural gift of making friends, we are all expecting great things from him in life. And so may life give you, Tracy, the happiness which you always try to give to others. With your debonair insouciance you can hardly fail to accomplish whatever purpose you may conceive.

name: George Britain Walton
motto: "To obtain success through the pursuit of the most worthy ideals."
description: George B. Walton, better known as "Shorty" came to the University in September, 1920, and has made an enviable record as a student. During his four years at Georgia he has made many friends, who will miss him when next September rolls around. Shorty is from Hamilton, Georgia. He is a hard, conscientious worker, always doing his best. He is now going to be rewarded with the coveted Bachelor of Science degree for his consistent efforts throughout the past four years. Shorty will leave Georgia with many things besides his diploma, however. He will take away the memory of many happy college associations, the lasting regard of his friends, and a truer perspective of life than he had when he came to us. We expect to hear great things of him in the near future, because a man of his personality and ability will not be denied. Shorty, your friends wish you every happiness in life and predict for you a fine success in your chosen work.

name: Alma Viola Ward
motto: "To be rather than to seem."
description: Alma hails from a city that has gone down in history as a habitat of things sweet, especially Georgia peaches and we find both her name and her "patriae urbs" admirably suited to her nature. In addition to being dulcis, Alma is one of the best students we have among the Co-eds, and is a convincing argument for co-education. She claims a championship too: She has never cut a class since entering college. "Al" is by no means lacking in the famous characteristics of Co-edism,—the ability to vamp, and sooner or later, all of us have succumbed to the merry twinkle of her eye, and the magnetism of her personality. But the main reason we all like Alma and predict for her a brilliant future is that she is an all round girl, a loyal co-ed, and a real friend.

name: Henry Latimer Watson, Jr.
motto: "Defeat is only for him who accepts it."
description: "Tody" came to Georgia four years ago and since the first Freshman Club dance, has been recognized as a man among men. Even the debutantes admit it. He has been the outstanding social leader at college and it is going to be hard to find another to take his place. Every boy that knows Tody likes him, and the girls fall for him on all occasions. It is stated on good authority that "he never fails". Tody has made as many real friends as any man in college, and at the end of four years of association many of us can say that here is a man truly worth knowing. He is possessed with those qualities that draw you to him, and those who know him best are confident for his future success and happiness. Tody, we all wish you the best of luck. With your rare personal magnetism you should have no difficulty in cracking the husk of life.

name: Joshua Lanier Watson
motto: "Accept thy good fortune without pride, resign it without reluctance."
description: Behold what the placid little city of Statesboro hath given to a fond world. Oh, sleep on somnulent commonwealth for thine has been a rich yielding to old "Georgia". His is a golden personality, his is a geniality that has made him friends without number. Friends that have avidly grouped around to hear his soothing, gentlemanly voice, pour forth platitudes, friends who have gone away the gayer for a few words of counsel and witticisms from "smilin' Josh". Josh's scholastic pursuits have been confined to "Little Charlies" ever proficient department. We do believe that Fortune will bring her reward in fullest measure to the feet of a man who has been universally a friend of mankind. Our athlete entered in 1920, after a proud Georgia Military College had sent him away laden with coveted honors. His four letters in baseball, his one in basketball, and oh, so much more, justify the faith of his mates.

name: Sarah Parks Weems
motto: "If we always face the sunshine, the shadows will fall behind."
description: With a sunshiny smile, a ready laugh and plenty of energy, Sarah has combined good scholarship with outside interests, and has made her two years at the University pleasant for herself and for her friends. Though she is just that height that is nearest a man's heart, she is mighty independent. We believe that life has much in store for her, both in the way of material achievement and in that more satisfying thing called happiness. At basket-ball games you have seen her At games of foot-ball and base-ball too. She's a jolly good person to be with, When you feel rather tired and blue. She has made a success at Georgia, In classes she has won a high grade, But this has not kept her, not in the least, From being just a sweet unselfish little maid.

name: Ruth Flanigan Whatley
description: Coming to us from G. N. and I. C. and about "steen" dozen Summer Schools, Ruth brought lots of good ideas with her. She has set us straight about just lots of things such as the length of a string and Ann's age. The best information she brought, however, is about the weather. She never misses that guess, for she says nothing. "Virtue has its own reward," so we are on the lookout for great things in her career. Already her "youth" has returned! Her beauty is of the fairest, and her wisdom never faileth. Her "B. S. H. E." will be honored for it will follow the name of Ruth Whatley. Ruth—just Ruth—always the same, dependable—when you want her she is there—jolly, serious, lovable—she's always your friend. "The purest treasure mortal times afford is spotless reputation." Count on Ruth and win. The way ain't sunny But don't you fret Cheer up honey We'll get there yet.

name: James Marion Adams
motto: "We think too much of what others think; too little of what we ought to do."
description: "Abie" has been with us since the fall of 1921. He is essentially a specialist. For those things he loves and in which he is interested he will work endlessly. He believes that there are too many branches of learning for one mind to master them all. He would rather concentrate on the thing he loves and try to delve deeply enough into that to reach the point where one feels like a "child gathering pebbles on a boundless shore." "Abie" has gained quite a reputation during his years of college as an amateur philosopher. Many will remember him talking enthusiastically until the "wee small hours" to a group of boys gathered in his room. We believe that you will always find life interesting, "Abie", for there will always be good books to read, thoughtful people to discuss new ideas with, and the outdoors in which to lose one's self.

name: Charles Roberts Anderson
motto: "As a man thinketh in his heart, so is he."
description: Shepherd is a character, as all of his friends will tell you. He is what most boys aspire to be, but which few attain. His gracious manner, his pleasing personality, and his undoubted intellectual ability have made him a man respected and idolized by his friends. No task is too small, no sacrifice is too great for him in the cause of friendship. If success follows him in the "Great Struggle" as it has in his college days, another great man's name will go down to posterity. The University loses a leader, a man of the highest character, and above all a modest gentleman.

name: John Lee Anderson
motto: "The determination to succeed is the first step toward success."
description: "Andy" is a native of Clarke County and proud of it. He is an example of the sturdiness of North Georgia stock, having been experimented on for three years at Winterville by the State College of Agriculture and exposed directly for four more years at the College itself. He is still living to tell the tale. He waited until his Senior year to wrest the debating crown from the veterans of the platform, so that he might take part in the Cotton School Debate. Strange as it may seem, for a gentleman of his pulchritude, John Lee is still "fancy free" and a power against co-education. We await with much anticipation the tremors which are sure to be felt when he makes his appearance on the gridiron of Life. Go to it, Andy! We are all for you!

name: Dorothy Katherine Barrett
motto: "And now abideth faith, hope and love, these three, but the greatest of these is love."
description: Here is one of the best pals for those who are fortunate enough to be numbered among her friends. To the casual she may appear reserved and indifferent, but don't let the first impression, or even the angelic look in the picture above deceive you. She is neither. However, Dot believes the world is full of opportunity only awaiting one's determination to seek it out and make use of it. "Psychology Shark" is one of the favored few who can make and keep up a "rep" for scholarship without studying, and moreover, attain beyond the usual in everything without the least indication of industry. As varying as the four winds, in her moods, she holds through all a steadfast belief in the efficacy of concentration as applied to all things under the sun. Just put sympathy, wit, comradeship, originality, and fun together and you have "Dot B." A true friend and a good sport.

name: Joseph Sprigg Beall
motto: "If not, why not."
description: "Farmer Beall" rode into town in the fall of 1920. Atlanta claims that he is a genuine son, as is her custom to claim citizens of worth, but the truth of the matter is that he was born in Cumberland, Maryland, many long years ago. This lad has the rare quality of thinking long before speaking, and then sticking to his decision. He is quiet, easy going, and makes the kind of friend that lasts. "Farmer" has developed into a perfect "Chicken Fancier" during his four years, having divided his time between Co-Eds and real poultry. It is said that he knows more about fowls than Wm. J. Bryan knows about monkeys. He has sought few of the so called "College Honors," but his work has been consistent, and he has succeeded upon a sensible basis. Farewell old man, luck to you!

name: Louis Neese Betts
motto: "Brains and Bull is a good combination, but if you must choose between the two—leave off the brains."
clubs: Second Lieutenant R. O. T. C. Infantry, 1922-23
Regimental Adjutant R. O. T. C. Infantry, 1923-24
description: Betts is an Athens product, and a representative one. He entered the University four years ago with the spirit to get all that was coming to him. This spirit of making good has predominated during all these years. He has fought a good, clean battle all the way through. He is a hard working scout, possessed of a modest, friendly disposition that has won him many friends. He has passed up his work most creditably and found time to engage in other college activities, making an enviable record in debating and military circles, as the above list will bear witness. It is conservative to say that Betts will make good in whatever field he chooses to follow.

name: William Lester Boone, D.V.M.
motto: "Look out for yourself as the other man will not do this for you."
description: Dan is a man who wishes to make friends with every one. He once decided he would get on good terms with one of his instructors and brought him a string of birds. As a result he came very near being kicked out of college. He is self-educated in some lines and has had experience in almost every field of life, including some experiences in the World War. He believes in looking out for himself and giving the other man the same chance. In Dan we have a lover of men, women and dogs. We are afraid to say which he likes best. Because he is human he has his own ideas and beliefs. Some things are natural, others are acquired, and still others are the gift of God. Dan, we wish you every happiness that one can have in life—business success, friendship, power. And, above all, that deep sense of satisfaction that comes only through some really worth-while service to humanity.

name: Nellie May Bowen, B.S.C.
motto: "To a valiant heart there is no discouragement."
description: You would not recognize in this Senior with all the proverbial dignity, the frivolous freshman of two years ago. Then it was the care-free, happy, light-hearted air; now it is the gentle, feminine dignity that charms us. You would hardly think that slender, lovely Nellie May was molded for the "cold" and "cruel" business world. Yet, for three years we have seen her, a zealous student of commerce, capable of doing well whatever she undertakes. In a contest once Nellie May was selected by the judges as the Co-ed with the prettiest, frankest, most winning smile. Our Alma Mater is proud to send forth one more daughter who has the will to do and to dare, and the courage to smile bravely thru life. She will take with her not only the best wishes for every success, but also the complete confidence of all her friends.

name: Hervey M. Cleckley, III
motto: "Hearts are dust, hearts' loves remain."
description: "Cleck" came to Georgia from Princeton in the spring of 1922. During his short stay he has achieved the best honors in school. When a student excels in the classroom as well as on the athletic field it is a sign of faithful application and great steadiness and strength of mind; when he convinces the people who know him most intimately, that he thinks—vigorously and independently, and always sanely,—along lines so various as to imply the widest range of interest—when he convinces these people, and most others, that they could not beat down their affection for him, however they might try, when he does all of this . . . . . well, this is what "Cleck" does. As a fitting climax of his achievements he has been chosen as Rhodes Scholar. This is the greatest honor that can be conferred on a student of an American college.

name: Clifford Warthen Collier
motto: "The father of success is work, the mother is ambition, and the oldest son is common sense."
description: Smiles greet Cliff where'er he ventures. The echo of his hearty peals of laughter reverberates throughout the expanse of the Oconee. He commands company and gets it, for he has injected more good cheer into the University than anyone who has graduated during the past four years. Cliff can well be called the best natured member of the class of '24. Meansville has indeed produced a son of whom she can boast, not only as a dispenser of happiness, good cheer, and optimism, but a producer in the truest sense of the word. He has been one of Dr. McHatton's star performers for some years and has made for himself an enviable record. He hopes to make "Collier and Son, Producers of Fancy Elbertas," the standard of perfection within a few years. Cliff, accomplished bull artist, horticulturist, and all round good fellow, you leave us with a feeling that we have known a man really worth while. Ladies, an eligible BACHELOR.

name: Martha Rebecca Clarke
high school: Georgia Normal and Industrial College at Milledgeville
motto: "Smile, and the world smiles with you."
description: Martha, a worthy follower of the Martha of old, finds her greatest joy in willing service. So faithfully and ably has she applied herself to her college duties that in one brief year she has been able to win the coveted reward,—a degree from the University of Georgia. Prior to her matriculation in this institution, she received a Home Economics diploma from Georgia Normal and Industrial College at Milledgeville. After that for a few years she rendered excellent service in the Home Economics field, both as an Extension Worker and as High School teacher. Her high achievement is in the culinary art, in which line may she continue to shine until some gallant swain persuades her that "He may live without love, He may live without books, But civilized man Can not live without cooks."

name: Lurline Collier
motto: "Character is the diamond that scratches every other stone."
description: Here's the easy going optimist from Jefferson—always peeping on the bright side of life. She has a cheering smile for everyone and her friends are numbered by the enrollments of students and faculty. In classes, she says but little, but when she does talk, she speaks wisely. She is a steady worker, earnest and straight-forward. The Freshmen declare she is a "shark" in Physics, but the Seniors say she is a "Josh" in Chemistry. Lurl's favorite expression is: "Why must studies conflict with one's College career?" Her keenest ambition is "to be tall." Her never failing sense of humor and the spontaneity of her wit are outstanding traits. Her fellow students wait in suspense, "While she winds up the watch of her wit, to see where it will strike." Lurline, our best wishes are really unnecessary, for you will attain whatever you strive for in this world.

name: Conger, Mary Elizabeth
motto: "Few things are impossible to diligence and skill."
enrolltime: entered in her Junior Year in September, 1922
description: Mary Elizabeth entered in her Junior Year in September, 1922, with a successful career and a record back of her of being fully capable and qualified for being a school "Marm,"—one to the manner born. In all her class work she has been prompt, faithful, and a tireless and efficient worker. Such perseverance together with sincerity and absolute dependability leads to success in any line of undertaking. Her ambition stands as a shining light of inspiration to her fellow-students. "Conky, the Punctual," makes some record—two degrees in two years and two months without an absence or a class cut. Her love of History and English, her experience as a traveler, her gift as an interesting and instructive lecturer, make it easy to imagine Mary Elizabeth Conger in the time to come, standing before large classes of students, lecturing as instructress in English or History.

name: Cook, Hugh Yandell
motto: "Let every man be occupied, and occupied in the highest employment of which his nature is capable, and die with the consciousness that he has done his best."
description: "Doc," as he is called by the boys, blew in sometime about the fall of 1921, whence, nobody knew. Of late the secret has leaked out and we have it from a reliable source that "Hugh Y," as the ladies call him, comes from Cooksville, Heard County, Georgia. The hero of this sketch came to the University as one of our World War Veterans, and has left a record behind him of which one should be proud. He has never taken a conspicuous part in any movement but his cautious judgment and deliberative conclusions have made him a valuable asset to any undertaking. He has been a steady but far from an over-zealous worker and therefore will leave our midst as many have done before him. "Our subject," believes that a chair is made to sit in, and that those with cushions are preferable. It behooves us, therefore, to leave "our subject" snug in the chair of ease well armed to meet the wiles of the world.

name: Robert Erie Cook, B.S.C.
motto: "The loud voice often scares but very seldom convinces."
clubs: First Lieutenant and Battalion Adjutant, '23, and '24
description: Four years ago there came a lad from Cottonwood, Alabama, to Athens, who was destined to become a wearer of the "red cap" on the Georgia Campus. Of course there were many good schools in Alabama, but "Bobby" had his heart set on the best, so he came to the University of Georgia. He came here with business on his mind, and as he goes into the world he chooses it as his profession. He has, of course, at times felt the pierce of Cupid's arrows—they all do. Bobby has made quite an impression upon all with whom he has come in contact, and his friends join in wishing for him, as he goes into the battle of life, a victory like unto that of Alexander the Great. We feel sure that when he is done there will be no more for him to conquer.

name: Jesse Wooten Cooper, B.S.A.
high school: Seventh District A. and M. School at Powder Springs, Georgia
motto: "When you call attention to the other fellow's faults, just remember that you are exhibiting one of your own."
description: Mr. Jesse Wooten Cooper, a product of the city of Armuchee, Georgia, came to the University in 1920 and joined the ranks destined to become the Senior Class of nineteen-hundred twenty-four. Cooper prepared himself for college at the Seventh District A. and M. School at Powder Springs, Georgia. He was an outstanding student in every phase of school life while there, and, since coming to the University, even though handicapped by the necessity of having to earn expenses while in school, he has made a record anyone should be proud to leave behind. In the Freshman year, Cooper won the scholarship offered by the Agricultural College to the Freshman having the highest scholastic standing. This gentleman has a winning personality and has a great many friends in college, but those who know him best are prepared to appreciate him most.

name: Walter Gerald Cooper, Jr.
motto: "Nothing is so congenial as a clear conscience."
clubs: Junior Delegate to Southern Federation of College Students
description: Walter Gerald Cooper, Jr., entered the University in 1920, following a brilliant career at Boys' High of Atlanta. Amply endowed with poise, dignity, and charm of manner, Walter has succeeded in every phase of college life without wavering in his devotion to his high ideals. He is a hard and consistent worker, a scholar in the true sense of the word, a true and loyal friend, and a gentleman of the very highest type. His classmates have rewarded his leadership in college activities by placing him at their helm in his Senior year.

name: David Wade Coppinger
motto: "Honesty, self-possession, determination, kindness, and confidence are the fundamental factors for an honorable success."
description: Copp was born in the mountains of Tennessee in the town of McMinnville. After spending nine years of his life in these hills his parents decided that it would be best to get the little fellow out in the open where he might see the sun rise and set. So in 1899 they moved to Atlanta, Georgia, where "Copp" entered the Formwalt Schools. Statistics show that he was a model pupil. On May 22, 1917, just after Uncle Sam decided definitely to lick Germany, he enlisted in the Army. He served with the Ninth Engineers Mounted, during the war, and was honorably discharged on December 24, 1919. He was an excellent soldier for his country. Copp, you are a little man with a great big warm heart. You are firm in your decisions, yet always open to conviction. To know you is to be your friend. We feel that you will succeed and be a credit to your Alma Mater.

name: James Fletcher Cox, A.B.
motto: "Manhood is above all riches, overtops all titles, and character is greater than any career."
description: "Governor" blew in from somewhere in the wilds of Northwest Georgia September, '23, with thirty hours of tentative credit and a great determination to finish college in one year. He seems in a fair way to do this ere the summer school of '24 has passed away. His pet study is French and till the wee hours of the morn he traverses the fields of French literature. He is a bright boy and will let neither time nor effort stand between him and accomplishment. He isn't much to look at from a standpoint of avoirdupois, but a great deal from the standpoint of blond curls and blue eyes. And he may have some weight in a debate. He has had little time to cultivate the social graces since coming to Georgia, yet he has made many friends who wish him much success. This isn't good-bye with Jimmie; he is coming back to take a shot at law.

name: Lemuel Emon Cox, B.S.Ed.
motto: "He that shutteth his lips is esteemed a man of understanding; and a wholesome tongue is a tree of life."
clubs: Adjutant of Joe Brown Connally Post D. A. V. of W. W.
description: Out of the Blue Ridge Mountains with their bracing air and pure water came "L. E.", a pure man. During his boyhood days he attended school, farmed, and taught school. Just as he had every wheel set to drive down to Georgia the ambitious Kaiser upset his plans. During the summer of 1918 Lemuel went over-seas and while in infantry action he stopped about fourteen pieces of flying shrapnel. When discharged from the U. S. Army Hospital he came to the University with the spirit to make good. During these five years he completed two years of High School, and four of College work, making grades above the average student. "L. E." has done much and said little. The adage, "What you think today is what you are tomorrow," holds true with him; for during his boyhood days his one dream was to become a great teacher. "L. E.", may success be yours.

name: Leonard Parker Dailey, B.S.A.
motto: "Cheer up, kid! You'll come out all right, for the nearer the dawn the darker the night."
description: Mr. Leonard Parker Dailey came to the University in the fall of 1920. His native home is Homer, Georgia. Dailey received his high school training at the Berry School, and as the boys from Berry have the reputation of making good in all lines of work, he has not proved an exception to the rule. During his stay at Georgia, Dailey has gone about his work in a quiet, unassuming way and has made a very creditable record. He is one of the fellows who would not be discouraged in securing an education, and has made his expenses while carrying on his regular college work. His major work has been in the Horticultural Division, and he has chosen Landscape Architecture as his life work. "Pete" Dailey, as he is known by his closest friends, has a bright future in his chosen profession. We believe in you, Pete!

name: Sallie Fannie Daniel, A.B.
motto: "No fool, no fun."
description: In the Fall of '19 a quiet but observant big Freshman of verdant hue entered Georgia from the sequestered vales of Danielsville. From her list of honors we see that she has become a "champeen" at tennis and other sports. As for her scholarship, she didn't hesitate to make Chi Delta Phi, Pioneers, and Zodiac, and took time also for her "Y" work. In her Soph and Junior years, satire and argument were her prevailing modes of warfare, but Senior year finds her in a more softened mood, with a kindliness of judgment for her neighbors' frailties and a sweetness of disposition which has gained for her many friends. Georgia, though proud of her handiwork, is loath to see her go. We wish for her the best of success and a long life of happiness.
