name: Auvergne D'Antignac, Jr.
motto: "Many a man has lost out trying to find a short cut to success."
description: Auvergne, or "Venus," as he is called about the campus by those who are close to him, is truly a man, worthy of the distinction he has won for himself in college circles. As Auvergne passes from the portals of the University he carries with him a host of friends. During his four year stay with us he has proven himself truly a great boy. Cool headed at times when those about him were in dismay, Auvergne has never lost sight of his aims. Now that his stay is finished his degree is but the material tribute of his work. In this man we find one of the happiest combinations possible,—an unselfish nature, a good mind and plenty of determination. Through close application and the consistent performance of his duties Auvergne will graduate as one of the best men in the school of Engineering. He will carry with him the best wishes of every man in his class.

name: Alfred Lee Davis
motto: "I will do well, if I strive towards, and aid my fellow men in attaining ever nobler ends."
enrolltime: finished a four year course in three years
description: Gentlemen, we have with us one of the hardest working of all the men whose likenesses you see among these pages. Davis hails from Rome, and he is the noblest Roman of them all. He has finished a four year course in three years and at the same time worked on the outside to finance his stay with us. He has not always made the highest grades perhaps, nor was he blessed with athletic prowess,—but Davis has been consistent. He has taken an interest in college activities, debates, and publications. We hate to see him go, but when he is battling against the odds of the world for some "sweet little thing" we wish for him health, wealth, love, and happiness. Georgia loses a good man, but our loss is another's gain, for at some future time, not long distant perhaps, Floyd County will proclaim him among her favored sons.

name: Theodore Roosevelt Day
motto: "Perseverance is the price of success."
description: "Roose" is from the city of Douglas, the home of the athletes. The 65-yard run he made against Auburn stamped him as a true son of his home town. To know Roose is to love him. He well deserves every honor that has been bestowed upon him; he has worked his way from the bottom. A truer heart never beat beneath a more manly breast. He commands the respect of all that know him, for he has always entertained and lived up to the highest ideals. In his short stay here he has made his boyhood dreams realities rather than mere hopes. It is unnecessary to mention his love for the ladies; he loves them all. "Roose,"—athlete, lover, big-hearted chap, we love you, and want you to be careful.

name: Harry Lee Death
motto: "Works of genius can be brought forth only by energy and industry."
description: Harry Lee came to the University in a very undecided state of mind because he had not received a favorable response from the Registrar to his request that he be allowed to pursue the courses leading to the degrees of Bachelor of Science in Commerce and in Agriculture at the same time. After tossing a coin to make his decision, he registered for Agriculture. After one year, he decided that the result of his toss was unfortunate, and for the past three years he has pursued relentlessly the subjects leading to the Commerce degree. Death certainly belies his gloomy name. His sunny disposition has made many friends for him among the students and faculty. He has given evidence of ability and diligence in mastering the detail work encountered in secretarial studies and accounting, as well as in all of his college work, and we predict for him deserved success in the commercial field.

name: Desse Donaldson
motto: "What I must do, is all that concerns me; not what the people think."
description: Here is a man who can not find the word "impossible" in his dictionary. Years ago, while still in his "teens" Desse acquired a thirst for knowledge, which, with his love of higher learning, and loyalty to his early ambition to be an alumnus of Old Georgia, has landed him in the front ranks of this year's graduates. He has accomplished in three years, what it takes an average man four years to do. He began with his class in 1920, and although his college course was interrupted by a year's absence, he has finished with his classmates. He is quiet, modest, dignified, and unassuming. He goes the even tenor of his way and pays as he goes. Desse has devoted his college years almost exclusively to the pursuit of knowledge, content with the assurance that honor and fame come to him who acts well on his part. We predict that the future will show that he is right.

name: Amelia Dornblatt
motto: "So much to do; so little done! But when it's over, the victory is won."
description: A vivacious smile, a cheery laugh, a winning personality, a deep understanding, fair to behold,—you have before you the living image of Amelia. She is the embodiment of all that is pleasing in woman, and of the reason why co-education is becoming so popular at Georgia. She was the first of the fair sex at the University to study Journalism, and from her class-room records, it can be seen that woman's sphere is everywhere. We look forward with pleasure to reading some work from her pen. She holds one distinction,—in spite of keen competition,—that is "championess" late-comer in history classes. Her record has never been equaled before, and doubtless, it never will. We hate to bid you farewell. We will miss you on the campus. Student of writing, may the Book of Life hold for you many First Editions,—and as many successes. May every page be filled with happiness.

name: Bernard M. Dornblatt
motto: "Life gives to you only what you give to life."
clubs: Lieutenant, Cavalry Unit R. O. T. C.
Captain, Cavalry Unit R. O. T. C.
description: Gaze, O Fair One, on the "great-hater" of your sex! And beware lest you lose your heart to him. For he is the one that you could easily admire,—modest, unassuming, true as steel, and in every word a man. "Jake" has made quite a record at Georgia as a speaker and a military man. As Captain of "C" Troop he has made it a successful leader. He will always be remembered for the stunning footgear he wore. When not on parade he was even spick and span enough to wear his "classy boots". The acquaintance with such a man as Bernard is what makes life worth living. Remember, Jakie, we shall never forget you and will remember you always as a gentleman in the true sense of the word. We are for you,—and we know you will succeed.

name: Annabel Dowdy
motto: "To be, not to seem; to do, not to dream."
description: Dootz came to us in the fall of 1922 from Agnes Scott, possessing qualities of self-assurance, independence, dignity, and ability. Consequently she immediately assumed and has retained command of our hearts and admiration. She was initiated into Phi Mu fraternity and has ever been one of its loyal assets. Due to her scholastic standing, her personality and ability, she was taken into Chi Delta Phi, an honorary literary society, in 1923. Her natural wit and charm are but two of her many attractive qualities. She possesses a gift for "arguing" and asserting her rights that has been unequaled. Easy going, yet determined; indifferent, yet dependable; austere, yet sincere; dignified, yet "peppy"; merciful yet just; popular, yet not conceited, she naturally holds a place in our lives which cannot be filled when she is gone. We are wishing for you the best that life holds.

name: WILLIE VIE DOWDY, B.S.H.E.
motto: "Because right is right, to follow right were wisdom, in the scorn of circumstances."
description: Although last year was her first, Willie Vie has won a host of friends that sincerely regret the parting words which must come in June. You can reason with her—if you have the time to spare, the wit to understand her, or the brass to try to bluff her. She is not only a most excellent student in the narrow sense of the word, but at times shows that she does not confine her thinking to text-books. Willie Vic has been blessed with her full share of the quality known as grit. No task is undertaken that does not receive all her untiring efforts, and is satisfactorily finished. Her favorite study while here has been Freshman Drawing, and her favorite expression is, "Anyhow, I don't know anything about it, myself." She has a friendly way and a pleasant smile which bid fair to win her every success in her after life.

name: ADOLPH EDWARD DREXEL, D.V.M.
high school: Second District A. and M. School
motto: "In aspiring to attain the Hall of Fame, Beware of ordainment to the Halls of Flame."
description: Ladies and Gentlemen, here he is,—a product of the blossoming, fertile plains of South Georgia. "Drex" came to the Classic City four years ago direct from the Second District A. and M. School and avowed his intention of becoming a Veterinary Surgeon. He has fulfilled his intention and is now a very efficient saver of souls. Not a preacher; not a missionary; not an M. C.; not a shoe cobbler; but,—a Veterinarian. When Drexel came to Athens he possessed much of the chlorophyll typical of the freshman, but believing in diligence, and economy of time, he zealously applied his energies to his work with early results. He is the second member of the Drexel family to hold Editorship of the Georgia Agriculturist, and the third Editor of this paper in the last six years coming from the Tifton A. and M. School. You are on the right road, "Drex",—push on.

name: William Van Buren Edwards, B.S.A.
motto: "To strive, to seek, to find, and not to yield."
description: Yes, folks, this lad came from the city of Griffin to our Northern Clime. The credit he has reflected upon his place of abode is outstanding, mighty, and worthy of recording. His keen sense of humor has established itself to a marked extent in the minds of his friends. Bill possesses certain traits of personality that have won for him a place of distinction during his four years stay here. When Bill wades through the vile mires of the bitterness in life, and throws his head, feet, and all to the four winds his friends should rest assured that he will be beginning on the paths of success that destiny has laid down for him. As Bill bids his class farewell, there is an assurance that the world, humanity, and civilization will profit by his success. Power and happiness to you, old man, and may Wall Street beware if you direct your efforts against it.

name: Slade Heyward Exley, D.V.M.
motto: "Be silent and safe; silence never betrays you."
description: The hills of Habersham gave to Georgia the wonderful Chattahoochee, but it was left to the swampy marshes of Effingham to produce Exley. It was hard for this salt-water Geeche to forget the language of his childhood days and learn to speak English, but after four years of effort we present to you the finished product. His line is so deadly and his aim so sure that he numbers his victims by his female acquaintances. The difference between Geeche and a great many other Sheiks is that he not only fans the flame of love within them, but he meets their every need as well. What we like most about him is his unwillingness to quit in the face of disappointments. He came here handicapped in a great many ways, but by determination he has overcome the last obstacle and now stands ready for a successful career.

name: Handy Bruce Fant
motto: "Success is the bride of Endeavor And Luck but a meteor's gleam."
clubs: Captain Infantry R. O. T. C.
Elijah Clark D. A. R. Essay
Walter R. Hill Prize in Ethics
Joe Brown Connally Scholarship in Georgia History
description: Another product of Athens High! From his long list of honors it can be seen that he was active in the University. Handy went in for everything: Athletics, scholastic honors, military fame, and literary laurels. Though a consistent loser in ten consecutive oratorical try-outs he does not regret his associations with Phi Kappa. Last Commencement he walked off with almost every prize in the catalog. His amiability, his frankness, coupled with his sterling character and high ideals have made him a well known figure on the campus. Such versatility must of necessity find an appropriate reward, even from a reluctant world.

name: Jesse Russell Farr
motto: "Think not that when a friend does you a favor he expects one from you in return."
clubs: State Commander Dept. of Ga. D. A. V. of W. W.
description: Due to injuries incurred in the service, Farr was sent by the Federal Board for Vocational Education, to the University of Georgia to complete his education. On September, 1921, he entered the department of Veterinary Medicine and by the end of his Sophomore year all entrance requirements were off. September, 1923, he registered Senior and will complete his work December 1924. He was elected Commander of the State Department of Georgia Disabled American Veterans of the World War at the State Convention held in Atlanta, March, 1923. He is also a member of the American Legion. Due to his student activities and rank as a scholar in his department he was elected to membership in the Fraternity of Alpha Zeta in November, 1923. Luck to you, Farr. The world is open to you. We are watching you and expect you to bring credit upon yourself and your college.

name: Clifford Eugene Farrar
motto: "Do the best you can as you see it, and pray that you haven't inherited defective eyesight."
description: Although "Geraldine" has not sought scholastic honors he has set a mark for consistency that others would do well to emulate. This statement is attested by the fact that he has carried off two degrees in four years. Along with his studying he has also found time for other college activities in a social and literary way. Possessed of a wonderful voice, "Geraldine" truly lives up to his nickname. He made the Glee Club in easy fashion. Genial, carefree, happy-go-lucky, and yet withal a thoughtful seriousness about him, he is the ideal "University Man." "Geraldine" is thinking seriously of corralling another degree before he gives up his books for the sterner tasks of life. There is no doubt that he will get his Ph. D. if he decides to study for it; for it is his nature to finish everything that he starts. Verily, he is a scholar and a gentleman.

name: Leila May Fears
motto: "Do your best and let the other fellow do the worrying."
description: Isn't it grand to be an Athenian! We cannot help but love Leila May more because she was born and bred in a college atmosphere. "Fears" is rather conscientious, so she started early in life to do her work well. She went through Athens High, graduating with honors. She then chose to spend the next two years of her life at G. N. I. C., where she made great records in both home economics and in showing the teachers how to do things. "Fears" came to us in the autumn of 1922, and started to work specializing in accounting. Besides being a wizard in this field, she has also attained recognition as an orator. She lays down such cold facts before you which you cannot hesitate to accept. Leila May, as you leave to enter the business world we hope the training you have had is just the beginning of your career and that you will reap the rewards of hard work and diligent study.

name: George Thomas Fesperman
motto: "A word warm from the heart enriches me or another."
clubs: Member of Glee and Instrumental Club, '23, '24
description: "Irish" came to the University a "little boy" and during his first year lived the life of such. He returned in September, however, a young man with much ambition and a definite purpose. Waycross High School boasts of "Irish" as her son. There he won quite a reputation for himself as an athlete and a student. Since coming to Georgia, however, his activities along those lines have been confined to a three year course in managership, reaching a climax last fall when he accompanied the Georgia Bulldogs to New Haven as manager of the football team. In the student body are many who really like George. His sunny smile, congenial disposition, and sincere enthusiasm for anything sponsored by his Alma Mater endear him to all who know him. Irish will reflect credit to the school he loves.

name: Dagma Leak Floyd
motto: "To always strive for that which is worth while."
enrolltime: fall of 1915, entered the Freshman Class
description: Floyd came to Georgia in the fall of 1915, entered the Freshman Class, had his hair cut, joined a fraternity, went out to S. N. S., and still found time to work. He dropped out to go overseas, and when he came back no handicap was great enough to make him lose courage and give up a college degree. Throughout his entire stay in college he has been a consistent winner of prizes. The tablet upon which his record is written, while not so flashy as some, is of the kind that never tarnishes or grows old. It is safe to predict that he will fill his mission well. He deserves great credit for the persistence with which he has gone after a college education. Such persistence will stand him in good stead when he encounters the trials and tribulations of life, and with it we predict his arrival in the harbor of success.

name: Joseph Harold Fowler
description: Well, Joe is a local product, and we are proud of him. He has elected Engineering as his life work and expects to build his own ladder, or bridge, or whatever one uses in climbing to fame and success. He can keep a transit line on the map fairly well, but when he starts "slinging his line" to the weaker sex he flies clean off the globe. Well, Joe, go to it, we are for you; just remember that it's the way you show up at the show down that counts. You have made many friends at Georgia who are confident that your smile will be as effective as your instruments in this anomalous existence. Life is too complicated to be resolved by any means more mechanical than a charming personality.

name: John Hilton Freeman
motto: "A promise made is a debt unpaid."
description: It is unnecessary to dwell on Hickey's scholastic and political conquests—his appalling list of honors speak for themselves; but it is his magnanimous personality that we are so vainly attempting to describe. His nature, like his massive frame, is broad and well nigh all-enveloping. As one of the Business Managers of the Pandora he has proven his worth in the commercial world, yet even amid the sea of figures, bad checks and multitudinous worries concomitant with that office, the spark of philosophy blinking now and then proves that his thoughts were not bound in the tedious, constricted path that the physical part of a Business Manager is forced to tread. Hickey is well prepared for his battle of life, and we are strong in our belief that he will not fail.

name: William L. Fulghum
motto: "Hope is a willing slave, despair is free."
clubs: Delegate Student Confederation Y. M. C. A.
description: It is difficult to give an idea of a very fine man with scarcely a hundred words. It is almost impossible to do him justice without using phrases which, through false association (in Pandoras and other College publications) with mediocre men, have lost their significance and are becoming commonplace drivel. In spite of this we hope our works will carry some meaning when we say that here is one of the very few people in whom we have felt discriminating intelligence; a mind free from the dead formulas which sway so many; and a rare hunger for the finest and best things in life. It will seem hackneyed when we say he is a true gentleman, that he really feels all there is in the word "honor," that he is sensitive, understanding, unselfish and sincere. But that can not keep it from being true.

name: Helen Gibbs
motto: "Life is made of dreams; civilization is built on dreams; never break the golden chain that binds your dreams."
description: Picture to yourself a fair young maiden reclining at ease in a rocking chair before an open fire and building air castles until she falls to sleep only to dream about those cherished castles. Then you have a pretty good idea of Helen Gibbs from Social Circle, who is the personification of everything that the name of her home town suggests. Always smiling, she is the very essence of sociability and has thereby made a friend of every one with whom she has come in contact. Helen came to us from Brenau and has spent two years at Georgia, principally in Peabody Hall, where she has fitted herself to teach the youth of Georgia and to bring the future citizens up in the way they should go. Remember your motto, Helen, and here's hoping that in the near future all your dreams will have become glorious realities. Success to you always.

name: Fred Mark Gorfain
motto: "The sacred duty of man is to enhance the heritage of his generation as a fitting estate to the next."
description: "Gora" is versatility personified. A man that can find the wherewithal to pay his monthly board bill and go through college at the same time, besides taking a far greater part in the life of the University than the average student, surely deserves one's admiration and esteem. Many able debating teams have fallen before his onslaught of words—words that fairly batter one's ears to shreds and convince far before his speech is over. "Gora," you have determination, inexhaustible energy, and a well defined notion of what is right. It is useless for us to wish you success—you are nearly there now.

name: John William Grant, Jr.
motto: "Since life is not endless, let us enjoy life while it lasts."
description: Back in 1919, in the days when the word sophomore struck terror to the heart of every Freshman, Chubby migrated to Athens from the "New York of the South," and donned the red cap for the first time. With the exception of a year and a half when he became an habitue of Old Nassau, The Rendezvous, etc., he has been with us ever since. Chubby's favorite amusement is "the game they play upon the links," at which he is quite an adept and is frequently to be seen roaming over hill and dale in pursuit of the small white pellet. But in between times he has found time enough to satisfy Dr. White, which proves that he is capable of coping with the cruel world. In spite of Chubby's pleasure-loving disposition, he can be serious when occasion demands, and we predict great success for him when he steps forth into the business world.

name: Mary Ratliff Gray
motto: "Whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap."
description: Mary Gray was left an educational orphan at the death of her Alma Mater, Cox College, and came to Georgia for adoption in September, 1923. She is a good student, conscientious and untiring in her effort to master every day's problems. Her original mind insures her against any great difficulty in the philosophical side of her studies, and unflagging determination has enabled her to master their technicalities. If there is a reward for the faithful, that reward will be Mary's. It is no uncommon occurrence to find her spending Saturday afternoon in the Botany Laboratory. She has enjoyed the lighter diversions of college life, but never at the expense of her real work. As in her school work, so in her life work she conquers daily the tasks life brings. It is with interest that we send her out into the world from whence she came to lead the youth of Georgia into the ways of knowledge.

name: Mack Hall Griffin
description: Mack Hall Griffin, conscientious worker, loyal friend, scholar supreme. Douglas, the South Georgia dome of Coffee County, cannot claim Herodotus or Gildersleeve as native sons; but here's a classical genius of whom she's proud. "Mack" prefers to master the ramifications of one Greek verb in true Bocock fashion rather than the whole gamut of Peabody Hall psychology. Though a philologist at heart, he hasn't failed to shine in all his work at college. When the whistle blows, "Mack" is always there to help Georgia win. Ere this you have noted that he doesn't blare a trumpet before him as he follows the modest, even tenor of his way; and maybe you are guessing what sort of voyage he is mapping out for himself. But be assured that his sail on the Sea of Life will be a credit to his Alma Mater. The Haven of Success be your port, "Mack".

But be assured that his sail on the Sea of Life will be a credit to his Alma Mater. The Haven of Success be your port, "Mack".

name: Flossie Almeda Hairston
motto: "'Tis little good you'll do, watering last year's crops."
description: A sterling girl in the true sense of the word, with a heart of gold. She came to us in the Fall of 1921, direct from the Normal School. Since she has been in college she has been a good student, but not a bookworm, by any means. Wherever she goes, she is generally in a big hurry, but usually gets there. "Fliss" as she is known by her friends, will stand up for what she thinks is right, and fight for it. She has a great number of friends and to know her better is to appreciate her friendship more. She is never too busy to play a prank, or lend a helping hand. Her generous nature and sympathetic spirit, combined with the above mentioned qualities, go to make her attractive to the extreme. "Fliss," we know that you will meet life squarely, and will be successful in whatever you undertake.

name: Esper Everette Hall
motto: "Just one taste of success is much better than a mouthful of defeat."
description: Reared in the hills of northeastern Georgia, he started his career as a real "dirt farmer," but laid it aside to help Uncle Sam whip the "heinies". After this he decided to raise chickens as a life vocation, so came to us as a poultry student. His present ambition is to produce a breed of hens that will lay two eggs every day in the year and be tender enough for eating after a long life of laying. "Uncle Bim" Chaffin tried to inoculate him with the Bacillus Politicus, but it failed to take (see Gorfain for further particulars.) A steady and hard working boy, Esper will do his very best toward making a success of any undertaking he may attempt. He is faithful to anything that tends to the betterment of Georgia and has shown by conscientious, faithful and untiring effort that his ambition runs high in the agricultural world.

name: Newell Martin Hamilton, B.S.C.
motto: "Things worth doing at all, are worth doing well."
description: Julius T. hails from the Pine Barrens of Thomasville, where corn pone and 'possums reign supreme; but these two articles must make excellent brain food for he has the added distinction of being a three year man. "Julius T" sometimes known as "Ham" has for two years been a member of Georgia's Golf Team, and as such was instrumental in their winning the Southern Intercollegiate team trophies in 1923. It has been rumored that he was persuaded to take up the game because he heard a member of the fairer sex say "he looked divine in Knickers." Be that as it may, it hasn't interfered with his ability to win trophies. As you leave to conquer the world of finance, and the heart of some fair damsel, carry with you enough of that satisfaction, that comes with having done your task well, to assure prosperity. Carry with you our sincere wish that Fortune may shower her choicest blessings on you.

name: LaBruce Mortimer Hanahan, B.S.C.
motto: "Life is yours—make of it what you will."
description: This young man, "Lefty", native of Dothan, Alabama, possesses all traits of a man. He entered the University four years ago, and from that time has taken life seriously, bearing in mind that all play and no work ruins the best of men. He has also kept the fact in mind that the reverse of this is equally as bad. "Lefty" is a great fellow personally, and, although he is quite young, he has the level head on him of a man in his forties. He posssesses much poise and that old ability to think quick in a pinch. Being a demon with the ladies is not the half of it, for he has by his wonderful disposition won many life long friends among his fellow students. There is little use to predict success for "Lefty," for he has it by the handle on a "down hill drag."

name: Felix Hargrett, B.S.C.E.
motto: "Stand either at the top or bottom, - be the best or the worst."
description: This is the only man of our acquaintance whose heart has never skipped a beat during his four years in Athens. It is said on good authority that some one in South Georgia has kept it all the while. This hasn't worried Felix much, though, and his record is an enviable one. According to one who knows, he spent more time on music than on work, and he sings the Sextette from Lucia with the facility that you and I would try to sing America. Withal, he has led his class in engineering for four years in addition to carrying much work in other departments and has found time to develop himself into a military genius. Truly, a man's college career has been successful when he has proven himself a musician, a scholar, and a gentleman. The best waits for you, Felix.

name: Harrold Milford Herrin, Ph.G.
description: Harrold, although just receiving his long sought sheepskin this yaer, is not a freshman among us for he was here in 1916 and '17, hearing the call to arms that Uncle Sam sent out, Harrold left the university and entered the service at Camp Wheeler. After staying there for several months he was sent overseas where he saw many months service and he truly proved a hero. It was in the service that he first rolled a pill, administering first aid to the doughboys that were not so lucky. He served several months in the A. E. F. as an ambulance driver. On coming back to the States he decided again to take up his studies at the university in the Pharmacy department. Good luck to you, Harrold; in the field you have chosen, may your success there be as great as the success you have had in your visits to Dr. Wilson's class room. We are for you, Harrold.

name: Wellborn Buchanan Hill, A.B.
motto: "Be as the moon; it sees all, knows all, and yet is silent."
description: The city of Newnan, Georgia, must be situated in a very "Hilly" section, because for several years, at short intervals, varied Hills have descended upon us from that fair city. The subject of this sketch has been with us for three short years, yet he has filled a niche in our lives that will seem conspicuously empty when he is gone. He has a heart as big as his frame, and that is saying something. On the few occasions when he has been able to neglect his pursuit of a degree long enough to devote any time to forensic pursuits he has shown marked ability as a speaker. Although he is far from being a tea-hound it is rumored that more than one feminine heart will be broken by his graduation and departure. Wellborn Hill is a true gentleman and will make Georgia a fine citizen. Our best wishes go with you, Web.

name: Alton Hosch, M.A.
motto: "So little done—so much to do."
clubs: Editor Y. M. C. A. Handbook
description: The above record speaks plainly for itself. Words can add little to actual deeds, but our class takes this opportunity to extend to Alton its sincerest wishes for future success and to place on him its deepest confidence. Mindful of his innumerable good qualities and capabilities, his friends smile at this past record, since they know that a better one is to come.

name: Hampton McNeely Jarrell, A.B.
motto: "To follow right for right is right, were wisdom in the scorn of consequence."
clubs: First Lieutenant R. O. T. C.
description: Wonder of wonders! Here is a person who has wit and restraint enough to avoid being a ladies' man, without the pseudo-asceticism of a self-styled misogynist; a person who can see in women something akin to human beings. Hampton has graduated from the University of Georgia in three years and has still taken occasion to think, to write, and to discover that a library is of some use other than as a repository for defunct parallels. Without resorting to the hypocrisy of cultivation, and without shamming a false conviviality, he has still made friends who are able to appreciate those qualities, which, though esoteric, are none the less attractive. Hampton has written serious verse, has read widely, has developed a poetic imagination, has, in short, achieved as near to a liberal education as is possible for college men. Above all, he has lived up to the ideals of straightforward manliness.

name: Joseph Gillman Jarrell, B.S.C.E.
description: It is not cleverness that produced the great buildings, bridges, and the railroads. It was hard, consistent, and intelligent work. Joe is a man that combines an aptitude for work with unfailing good humor and a truly generous disposition. His sincere enjoyment of a good joke and his lively enthusiasm, for anything, have made for him close friends among his associates. If you want to see a football game fought and won, watch him. No player on the field works as hard for victory, nor suffers so keenly in defeat. Here you see that moving Georgia spirit that has made our student body famous. Luck to you, Joe, and may you be as good an engineer as you have been a student. You will leave behind you, when you fare forth into the world many friends whose best wishes will follow you wherever the vicissitudes of life may take you in the years which are to come.

name: Lyons Barnett Joel, B.S.C.
motto: "Make the world a little better off for having the privilege of living in it."
description: Another one of our three year men who besides making the Commerce course in these three has made most of the important clubs of the University that have to do with social life. Soon after he entered in the fall of 1921, his talent as a musician was noted. He contributed for that year and the next two years to three of the best Glee and Mandolin Clubs in the history of the University. That this talented and brilliant young man will make a big success in his later life as in college is the sincere wish of his friends. It is even more than a wish; they feel sure that all the fine things prophesied by his college record will come true. With his charm of manner, his optimism, and his ability to make real friends, we feel safe in predicting happiness for him in life that will equal his success.

name: Howard Love Johnson, Ph.G.
description: All hail to Wrightsville! Love is his middle name and believe me he sticks to his name. He is a demon on the dance floor as well as in the class room. He is one of the most popular young men in college, and has made many honors during his stay here. We do not have to comment on his looks, for you can see he is a sheik by his picture. He will continue his study of medicine in Augusta next year, so if you need a good Doctor don't miss this chance. We all would certainly like to know why he makes so many trips to G. S. C. W. Say, Love, just what is the attraction? All the luck to you in the broad field of your chosen profession. We are quite assured of your success if you continue your studies in Augusta as you have at Georgia.

name: Jefferson Brooke Johnston, B.S.C.
motto: "There is always life for a living one."
description: "Whole-hearted, true-hearted, faithful and loyal." What more could be said of anyone? It is as near as we can come to portraying Brooke. "We rise by the things that are under our feet; by what we have accomplished of good and gain." So take a rare ambition with unrivalled energy, put them into a highly esteemed character, and you can see how Brooke has risen in our ranks. Brooke has held many positions in the University, and throughout the performance of them has shown that competency and ease of execution that distinguished him as a leader. He is also one of those fortunate persons who create an atmosphere and yet have a personality that is singularly sensitive to the temperament of others.

name: William Gibson Johnston, B.S.A.
motto: "Opportunities do not present themselves; they are home made."
description: Bill came to the University some four years ago, as freshmen do from time to time, and even as you and I, he was immediately smothered by the Lordly Sophs. It was not long, however, before this son of Thomaston, land of Peaches and Sunshine, fought his way upward to the foremost rank of his class and now on the eve of his graduation he finds himself the possessor of many of the coveted honors which come only to the deserving. Bill has ignored the ladies while here, and we have a sneaky feeling that it is on account of his abhorrence of having someone sit on his lap and rumple his hair. Here's to you, Bill, may your journey down the pathway of life be pleasure always, and may all your ambitions be realized.

name: Harris Jones
motto: "If you manage to keep your own respect, you needn't worry about that of others."
clubs: President of Southern Intercollegiate Golf Association
description: Harris hails from Athens, but received his initial "kick-off" in education at Episcopal High School, Alexandria, Virginia, where he made an enviable record both in athletic and scholastic lines. Although Harris is only a three year man he has maintained his good record in college, and in addition has become a leader in social and golf circles. He puts an almighty wallop behind his golf club as has been demonstrated in golf tournaments in which he has participated. He has proved his ability as a leader by piloting to victory the Georgia Golf Team in the Southern Intercollegiate Tournament at Knoxville in 1923. Those who know him can scarcely resist the influence of his magnetic personality and there is no doubt that Harris will achieve, in his journey through life, the phenomenal success which his sterling qualities insure.

name: Henry Alton Jones
motto: "Act well thy part."
description: "Big Jones" is one of those steady, quiet, unassuming fellows whom you can depend upon on all occasions. He does not advertise his abilities or boast of his learning but when results are to be weighed and achievements measured, he is always among those present. Modesty, dignity, integrity of character, and unalloyed hard work are some of the qualities which have won for Alton a permanent place in the confidence and esteem of his fellow students. One of his noblest characteristics and greatest assets is his loyalty to his friends. Optimism pervades his whole personality, and aside from the fact that he is to take up dentistry as a profession, the term "down in the mouth" can never be fittingly applied to him. We feel sure that with his habits of consistent work, his tendency to look on the bright side of things, and his unfailing determination he will meet with success.

name: Joseph Mabbett Jones, A.B.
motto: "Deterrent obstacles lose their force when ambition is the driving force."
description: Joe came to the University of Georgia in the fall of '21 from Emory. His scholastic standing from that time until he finished last Summer is one that would make any scholar proud. In fact, the figure looks more like a dollar's worth of German marks than a Senior's average mark. But figures don't lie, and Joe's record stands as one of the highest in recent years despite the fact that he finished in two years, after his one year at Emory University. Joe finished ahead of his class in last Summer School, and immediately took up the study of law at Harvard University. He will, however, receive his diploma along with his regular class in June, 1924. Judging the future by the past, it is not too much to predict that Joe will make a wise and eminent jurist, of whom Georgia can be justly proud.

name: Lila Jones, B.S.H.E.
motto: "To thine own self be true; thou canst not then be false to any man."
clubs: Senior Representative of Girl's House Council, 1924
description: When Lila graduated from G. N. I. she had a purpose to do something great. This she realized in 1922 by coming to Georgia. It did not take long for Georgia to feel Lila's presence, for she soon announced on the campus her platform—To make a maximum record,—and this she has done on minimum work. Nobody ever sees Lila bone, but everybody knows she gets by "On High." To do this requires the qualities of quick perception, excellent memory, indomitable will, and a healthy supply of "nerve fibré". These she possesses to such a degree that there is nothing she cannot do, from mastering in three hours a term's work in Hawk's Chemistry, including eighteen Amino Acids, to edging her way through the fast closing door of the Cafeteria at 8:16 A.M. Lila can do anything, anywhere. She holds success in life in her hands.

name: Robert Bruce Jones, A.B.
motto: "Be not afraid to say no."
description: "B. J." is one of those hard workers who has through consistent effort completed his college course in three years. He is destined to be a lawyer, and a good one unless all signs fail. Bruce has a fine personality; he is quiet and gentlemanly, just the kind of person to invite confidence. He has many friends who will miss him when his college course is over. "B. J." is not a ladies man though let it be said here that it is from choice only and not from necessity. When and where he does "step out" he is ranked above them all, a position for which all of us envy him. He appears to be also a lover of travel, or is it business? Anyway, he keeps the rails hot between Athens and Macon. Bruce seeks for the best and if things are given in this world in proportion to worth, he will receive the best.

name: George Harris King, B.S.A.
motto: "It does not pay to worry, unless you worry for pay."
description: Here is one book agent who admits that his success in life was not garnered by traveling the dusty road with his satchel in hand. In fact, his utterances are so belligerent toward the book agent business that we dare not quote him. During his absence from school, King contributed materially to the upbuilding of the State by teaching. His ambition still runs along the lines of education, and in order better to equip himself he is majoring in Agricultural Education. In spite of the fact that King's dome is as void of hair as a snake is of hips, he is quite a ladies man. It is very doubtful if he will be found traveling the highway of life alone very long after he has finished his course here. You have chosen a good work, and may you some day realize more than a material success.

name: Joseph William Kling
description: Now here is something different, a person, alas, who does not "number his friends by his acquaintances," who does not depart the University with lofty ambitions and induced visions of success, and whom "to know" is not necessarily "to love!" Poor youth, if he be so extraordinary, it is to be feared that he is a dirty radical—even a menace to the common weal! Could it just be proved now that he had ever seriously written poetry, and that he did not go to Sunday School—both plausible suspicions—deportation would be too kind a measure for such unprecedented iniquities. The culmination of his sins is really unmentionable: he thinks much, and has ideas—not ideals, mind you—a strange faux pas in this conventionalized neighborhood. But Joe is a literary asset to the University, without being a debater or declaimer; a scholar without reclusion; and even a gentleman, in spite of his not attending dances and not having ideals.

name: William Taylor Knight
motto: "Men who marry and bring up large families are of more service than those who only talk about population."
enrolltime: During his three years' stay at the University
clubs: Editorial Staff of the Commerce News
description: Here is a son of whom Quitman can well be proud. Imbued with a happy disposition which has endeared him to the hearts of scores of friends; frank, loyal, and generous to the core, he has well merited the honors which they have seen fit to bestow upon him, as an indication of their friendship and esteem. During his three years' stay at the University he has completed the whole four year course in Commerce. Until Christmas of his last year, he was firm in his determination to seek his fortune in a foreign clime, but Dan Cupid knocked at his door and sent a whole sheath of arrows into his heart. Under these conditions he will have to remain with us. A good student and the best of fellows, his friends wish to him fortune's kindest smiles, and rest sure in the belief that he will eventually carve his name upon the hall of fame.

name: Shelby Preston Langston, B.S.C.
motto: “Lay well the foundation upon which a life worthy of imitation may be built.”
description: If there are any men in the University more widely known and more agreeably known than Shelby we miss an official guess. Coming from Monroe, Walton County, to study the theoretical side of business, having been previously experienced in the practical phase of it, he has succeeded nobly in his efforts. During his stay in college he has been a steady student and a man to whom others look up. His ability of making friends is shown not only by the number that he has in college but also by the number of them in Athens. He is an ideal male Cleopatra—in love one week and out the next—we are wondering who his next victim will be. The business world holds a great future for this young genius of Commerce. When he does get started, look out Henry Ford.

name: Eli Lanier, B.S.C.E.
motto: “What I want is some good advice—on how to be naughty, and yet to be nice.”
description: For four years Metter, Georgia, has mourned the loss of one of her foremost citizens. “E” Lanier’s ambition to become an engineer was responsible. As will be implied from his philosophy of life, he has made his visits to the Co-ed Barn and his calls on the fair sex frequent. It may be that some fair little damsel has mercilessly snared his heart at some unsuspecting moment—alas! Who knows? At times his eyes are dreamy, and his actions seem passing strange. Still we believe that “E” will be a good provider and whoever she may be will be indeed fortunate. “E” has an uncanny ability to make friends wherever he goes, and it is no small number at the Universitas Georgiae who wish for him the best of luck and the kindest smiles of fortune as he goes forth to battle against the odds of destiny.

name: William McKinley Lanier, B.S.C.E.
motto: "Danger lurks in trodden paths while fame comes to those who fear not to walk in regions unexplored."
description: Bill came to Georgia in 1920 as a freshman, inspired with the thought of some day taking his place among the leading engineers of the nation. He became a disciple of "Little Charlie", who, by careful grooming has made it possible for Bill to peer over the brow of the hill into the land of his desire. It is true that Bill started as a chain-man, but he has risen from the ranks, and today he is one of those upon whose broad shoulders the future of the nation's engineering rests. Of course Bill has his weaknesses, but who wouldn't when Mother Fate was so kind as to tempt him with the fairest of the fair? It is rumored that he will not long remain single after commencement. To know Bill is to be acquainted with a good fellow and a true gentleman. He always has a smile for everyone he meets.

name: Edward Percival Lawton, Jr., B.S.C.
motto: "Satire's my weapon; but I'm too discreet To run amuck and tilt at all I meet."
description: In the two and a half years Ed has been at Georgia he has accomplished more than the majority of us have in four. This is shown by the fact that this year he was chosen student instructor in Economics. He has also won fame as an essayist, debater, and orator. How he has found time to take part in so many college activities and still have time to read voluminously and write for the various college publications is a mystery to the rest of us. He speaks English, Il parle francais, et habla espanol, el uno as bien as l'autre. Besides doing all this Ed has made many real friends who are confident about his future, because Ed is of the material from which success is made.

name: Charles Julian Lovvorn
motto: "The word can't has no place in a man's vocabulary."
description: This rosy cheeked youth claims Carrollton as his native heath and this metropolis may consider it an honor to have such a son. Tootie first set foot on the U. of G. campus four years ago and since that time he has been a familiar personage here. His friends are numbered by all who have had the pleasure of knowing him. He has taken an active interest in his college and has lent his efforts to all her activities since he first donned the red-cap his freshman year. Tootie has also been very attentive to his studies during his four years here. His record is one to be proud of. It is the result of ability and diligence combined. We are all sure that Tootie will have a career in business just as brilliant as his record here. He goes out with the best wishes of all his friends.

name: Jonothan Lucas
motto: "If any man can convince me that I am wrong, gladly will I change, for I seek the truth."
enrolltime: three years sojourn at the University
description: After his assistance in dethroning the Kaiser, and after touring the Southeastern States appraising manufacturing plants and inspecting vegetable oils and high explosives, "Luke" dropped anchor at the University of Georgia. He soon decided to learn the art of penning his thoughts, and to accomplish this end he plunged into the pool of journalism and by degrees has become thoroughly saturated with the content of this stygian pond. Press, beware! When this scribe in the making is turned out into your fold the whole journalistic profession will be revolutionized. Jonothan is a very unusual man in many respects. During his three years sojourn at the University he completed one year's work at the College of Agriculture, and a four year course of Journalism. He has neither aspired to political ambitions nor has he envied those who obtained such honors. We wish you success Jonothan, though our wishes are unnecessary, for you will succeed in whatever you will to do in this life.

name: Lund, Maxfield
enrolltime: completing the four year course in three years
description: Stop! Look! Listen! Cast your eyes, look, gaze, behold, view, scrutinize, examine, ope your gleaming orbs! and you will see one of the gifts of the gods. This is one of the most bashful and unassuming students on the campus. Maxfield is truly a student of the old school, completing the four year course in three years and is now looking for new worlds to conquer. He will make his mark in the world and leave a very deep impression thereon when the world becomes older by a half century and we are enabled to look back upon the life history of this man. No doubt we will note accomplishments which are not present in the life of the average man. The University of Georgia has been enriched by the presence of this gentleman from the classic city. Go to it, Max, we are watching you and wishing you every success.

name: Merritt, Thomas Edward
motto: "When I die, I want to leave my soul on the earth, extending and growing."
clubs: Winner of Cracker Short Story Contest, '23
description: Farmer, soldier, actor, author, artist, caricaturist, journalist, orator, humorist, defender of science, leader, accomplished hobo, and even lover. He has contributed materially both with pen and brush to every one of the student publications at the University, his greatest accomplishment along this line being the founding of the "Southern Drawl." Tommy has never had to resort to politics for the honors he has obtained, but he has acquired them, as his name indicates, through sheer "Merit".

name: Rosemary Stevens Whitaker, B.J.
motto: "Cut today! For tomorrow they may cut you."
description: Rosemary will leave behind her an indelible memory of a good disposition, a happy-go-lucky air, a big heart, and a ready smile. She is in every way a "good sport." When we begin to analyze these generalizations, however, we are struck, for there are so many places from which one can start. First, she was initiated into Phi Mu fraternity soon after her arrival. It wasn't very long before her friendships extended way beyond the circle of her frat sisters to all the girls and boys on the campus. Having among her other attractive qualities that of being a good dancer, she rapidly became a favorite. Due to her personality and her ability as an actress she was taken into the Thalian Dramatic Club, and starred in the play, "Mrs. Temple's Telegram." She was one of the first members of the Girls' Glee Club, which was organized in January, 1924.

name: Durand Watson Whitmire, M.S.A.
motto: "To live, not for what I can get, but for what I can do."
description: In the fall of 1920, there came to us from Jackson County, a robust, handsome young man, who had done his bit in the late war. "Dude," as he soon came to be called by his many friends, came for what he could get that would aid him in furthering the cause of humanity. He was an excellent student. Although he did not seek honors, he leaves the shrine of this institution with an honor that means more than all others; that is, the friendship of all who knew him. His ability is shown by the fact that he is getting his Masters degree in four years. As he goes out to take up his work of aiding his fellowman, and shaping society towards ever nobler ends, we bid him Godspeed, and look, not for failure, but the crown of success. The reward of such altruism is the knowledge of a life well spent.

name: Whitner, John Selby
description: 'Twas a bright September morn of the year 1920 when Johnny made his appearance at Georgia, bereft of all his golden locks and adorned with the scarlet headdress. For four years he has done his work faithfully and prepared himself for the life upon which he is now about to enter. Though very diminutive in statue, he's a wonder when it comes to action. Short, wiry, active, and full of life—Johnny is a good all round athlete. Besides doing well in his scholastic work and being one of the mainstays of his fraternity in football, basketball, baseball and track, he has also taken a part in the social life of the University and has made for himself a body of close friends who will stand by him through thick and thin. Johnny is a man of sterling character and extraordinary ability. We hate to see you leave us, and our best wishes go with you, Johnny.

name: Wilson, Steven Alfred
motto: "Real happiness comes only to him who performs his duty."
description: An able student, a conscientious worker, and a well read man—that, ladies and gentlemen, describes Steve. He is willing to make any sacrifice in order to attain a worthy end. He believes that one gets out of a thing only that which he puts into it; and in consequence, everything that he undertakes, from the simplest to the hardest task, always received the same careful preparation. His ambition is some day to sit in the United States Senate. Anyone who, before knowing him, might think it an idle dream, would realize if they met him that he is likely to reach his goal. His ambition is a laudable one, not the vaulting ambition that o'erleaps itself. It is characterized by that steadfastness of purpose that he has exhibited so well during his college career. May he find a sufficiency of wealth, favor in his true one's eye, and a life full of good deeds.

name: Manning Stevens Yeomans
description: The above more or less handsome young man is called "Goat" not because his countenance resembles that of a Billy, but because he growls and "maas" like the domestic animal when peeved. "Goat" claims Dawson as his home, but he spends most of his spare time around Cornelia, Georgia, where his father owns an apple orchard and where, it is said, a very interesting young lady lives. Goat has distinguished himself in many ways at the University, chiefly as a most conscientious member of the Student Council during his Senior year. He is a very diligent student, a good sport, and an "all round good feller" personally. Although he has been studying agriculture and horticulture and poultry it is rumored in some quarters that he will eventually wind up as a stock broker. But whatever trade he chooses, we feel sure of his success. The best wishes of his classmates at Georgia will always follow him. Maybe he'll be a Circuit Judge like his father. Who knows?

name: Nellie Garbutt Young
motto: Make every minute count.
description: O! lovely Nellie, it's you I love the best! If fifty girls were round you, I'd hardly see the rest. Be what it may the time of day, the place be where it will, Sweet looks of Nellie, they bloom before me still. A beloved classmate, an admired student, and a leader in all activities, Nellie has gone joyously forward carving her particular niche in Georgia's Hall of Fame. Between studying for an A.B., writing a math thesis, and teaching Rehabs, she has found time to become President of everything but—the "G" club. Yet, if in the future she aspires to the athletic, who can deny that she may attain even that. When she has left Georgia for a wider field the impress of her personality and the results of her achievements will remain an inspiration and an ideal for those who follow her.

name: Youngblood, Claude Ralph
description: Industrious, persistent, quiet, unassuming—these epithets should give one a graphic impression of "C. Ralph." This Augusta boy has been at Georgia only two years, but in this short time has worked sufficiently hard, and amassed enough credits to obtain his diploma with the supplementary aid of a final year's work at Columbia. Unlike some, Ralph has not attempted the sensational and has been satisfied to remain in that sphere which is free from pseudo-fame and notoriety. For this he is to be complimented. Do not grasp the conclusion that Youngblood has contented himself with a position of mediocrity, as just the reverse is true. In refraining from self-exaltation, Ralph has made many friends, and, at the same time, has done a brand of work of which he might well be proud. His good records in his classes exemplify the type of work he does in all things, and presage success and service to be imminent.

name: Space, Phillup
description: Although Phillup Space is the title by which this gentleman is listed on the Registrar's books, his host of friends call him affectionately Pithecanthropus. His good looks are only exceeded by his charming personality, which is only exceeded by his speaking ability, which is only succeeded by his sheiking propensities. Mr. Space registered in the University together with his brother Phillup More Space and, though their stay with us has been short, they have already become the most prominent and popular members of their class. These boys number their friends by Mr. Dudley's absence list and are delicious and refreshing. We predict for Space a large place in the world and prophesy that he will be found everywhere. To know him is to love him, and to be numbered among his friends is a thing of beauty and a joy forever. Although you are leaving us, Phillup Space, you will remain in the heads of most of us.

name: Adam Leopold Alexander, LL.B.
motto: "He was a lawyer, not a robber, and all the people wondered."
description: It is rather doubtful in the minds of his contemporaries just when the Leaping Leopold made his presence known at the University. He did not seem to like the Academic Department, so he left us to try the Old Dominion and take a fling at law. His stay there was short, however, as he had heard what a wonderful Law Department there was here with his good friend "Silvy" at its head. Much of the time that Leaping has spent in college has been given over to the fair sex. We hear that one of his specialties is robbing the cradle. At the dances we always find him. In this department he is a regular Chesterfield. Once Leap gets his love affairs out of his head he should be one of the leading legal lights of our State. May you always have the best of luck in everything.

name: William DeLacey Allen, LL.B.
motto: "Know your case; and be honest, not only with your fellow-man, but also with yourself."
description: This is the Law School's nominee for Georgia's Hall of Fame. William DeLacey Allen; patriot, athlete, orator, scholar, lawyer, journalist, humorist, politician, gentleman, friend. Versatility, thy name is DeLacey. Lace, an honor man of his class, as well as its President, has not merely participated, but has been an unquestioned leader in every phase of college life. From the diamond on Sanford Field to the Halls of Demosthenian this man has so impressed his real worth and ability upon his associates, that it is no exaggeration whatever to say that Lace has more admirers and real friends than any other man at Georgia today.

name: Joseph Johnston Bennett, Jr., LL.B.
clubs: Capt. Cavalry R. O. T. C.
Capt. Pistol Team R. O. T. C.
R. O. T. C. Pistol Record '22
Vice-President Student Confederation Y. M. C. A.
description: All the men who played through that heart-breaking football season of 1923 will agree that Joe proved himself the greatest Captain that ever led a Georgia team on the gridiron. He was a rare combination of athletic prowess, strength of personality, and that intangible thing called leadership. We are sure that the same dominant spirit and aggressive personality combined with the truly bright mind that is yours, Joe, will carry you to a high and prominent place in life.

name: Francis M. Bird, A.B., LL.B.
motto: "Sincerity is the only foundation for a genuine personality and true friendship."
description: Buster's character shows strongly in his face. A confidence man with such a frank, open countenance could persuade Henry Ford to equip each flivver with lightning rods. A young lady of Athens once remarked that she believed Buster had never kissed a girl. We refrain from comment. Socially Buster ranks ace-high. Many of the young ladies on Milledge and Prince will regret his passing, and remember him long. Girls, however, make up only a small part of Bird's long list of friends. And truly, those who know him best love him most. Buster acquired an A.B. degree in 1922. This year he graduates from the Lumpkin Law School. Before gracing the bar it is likely he will spend a year at Harvard. From such a learned son, Old Georgia expects great honor and much fame.

name: William Angus Brown, LL.B.
motto: "Always be true to the best that is in you."
description: Gus, the college set of the metropolis of Dewey Rose, is a splendid example of the value of college association for "Angus dear," unlike "Gloomy Gus" of the comics, may well be called genial Gus. His ready smile, his hearty greeting of good cheer, and his love of fun have made Gus a most likable fellow and have enriched him with a host of friend. If an educated man is really one who has many points of contact with his fellow men, with a minimum of friction, then Gus is surely cultured, for he is at the top of this class. This handsome young gentleman has been too busy mixing with the fellows to have captured scholastic honors; but Gus is a consistent worker and has made a good record in his pursuit of legal wisdom. His aims seem to be to excel in some things, but to be good in all. Gus has always succeeded in this, and will continue to do so in the future.

name: Samuel Alfred Cohen, LL.B.
motto: "I believe in one God, one Country, and one Flag, to which I pledge my whole support."
description: The books of the University of Georgia do not record a more splendid achievement than that of Samuel A. Cohen, and certainly the law department has no student of whom it is more proud. For Samuel has by his own persistent efforts, acquired an intimate knowledge of the law that others might well envy, and by his genuine friendliness he has made a host of friends in every department of school. Samuel's ideals have always been of the highest, and he has ever striven to be worthy of his ideals, living a clean, conscientious life worthy of the emulation of every one. If we could be as true to our friends, to our duties, and to ourselves, as is Samuel, then this old world would be happier to all concerned. Samuel, may life's richest blessings be showered upon you and may you succeed in everything you attempt.

name: Henry Woody Davis, LL.B.
motto: "Strive, but deal fair with your fellow-man."
enrolltime: After the close of the late war
clubs: President Henry W. Grady Speaking Club
description: After the close of the late war, Woody, as he is known at home, decided upon a career as a lawyer, and entered the law school of the University of Georgia as the first step towards the fulfillment of this ambition. His success in his legal studies is shown by the fact that in his second scholastic year he was one of the few out of a very large class to pass the State Bar Examination. During his stay here in the University he has experienced both sunshine and the sorrow of life; but fair weather or foul, his hearty greeting and friendly smile have ever been the same. The many friends he has won here at college, appreciating both his legal aptitude and sterling character, prophesy for him a life full of success and happiness wherever he may go.

name: Thomas Carlyle Dyar, LL.B.
motto: "Worry not over the future, the present is all thou hast, for the future will soon be present, and the present will soon be the past."
description: In Carlyle we find one of the most substantial members of the Law class of '24. His type is not that of a flashy genius, but rather that of a reliable man who is possessed with an abundance of common sense. While not conspicuously brilliant Carlyle has never failed in a subject and his marks are excelled by few, if any, members of the class. It would hardly be correct to say of him that he is a student, for in reality he is already a lawyer, and a finished lawyer, capable of gracing the bar of any state. To speak of Carlyle's scholastic attainments would certainly cover only a small part of his achievements. He has proven himself worthy of the strongest of friendship and truly he has amassed a host of friends during his stay at the University. Carlyle has woven himself into our hearts and it will be a sad parting on Commencement Day but it is with greatest assurance that we say that we feel sure that we shall always consider it an honor and a privilege to be called his friends.

name: William Marcus Fulcher, Jr.
motto: "Lay well the foundation; that others may view the structure."
description: In the year of 1920 "Bill" came to the University from his domicile in the little town of Waynesboro. His "permanent wave" and "dominating ways" immediately gained him many friends, especially among the fairer sex. He has been an outstanding social leader and worker, which has gained him untold popularity. Beside his social activities, he is one of the outstanding men in his class, having been President his Sophomore year, and making a high average in his work. He has a winning personality which will be of intrinsic value to him in his after life. He is always ready to recognize the good qualities in an acquaintance, and rather lenient toward one's weaknesses. As "Bill" leaves his large circle of friends to take up his life's work, we all look forward to and believe in his success.

name: Thomas Little Glenn, Jr.
motto: "Let a man contend to the uttermost, for his life's set prize, be what it will."
clubs: Cracker Staff Vice-President Jeffersonian Debating Society
description: Tom leaves us with the same smile which beamed over his countenance, when a great big, rough sophomore, in the fall of twenty-one, relieved him of his locks, and he began the battle with a clean pate as well as a clean slate. When he turns from us to face the cold, grim world, his determination to win shall not be without reward. His smile, never to be erased, is a characteristic symbol of future greatness. A poise, never lost, is the first notch in the ladder leading to the heights of success. He means harm to no one, but has abundance of "Irish" for those who wish him ill, so for the benefit of the writer's posterity, good health and happiness and because we feel it a conscientious duty to be honest, we predict a future of unlimited success and honorable wealth for our beloved friend.

name: Spence Monroe Grayson
high school: United States Military Academy at West Point, and Georgia School of Technology
motto: "Smile and the world smiles with you; weep and you weep alone."
description: After attending the United States Military Academy at West Point, and Georgia School of Technology, Spence came to us in the fall of 1920. His schooling at these different institutions has given him a broad view point which is noticed by his associates in all that he does. Spence's prowess as an athlete is not exceeded by his good looks but it pales before his ability as a lady killer. Although Spence claims Savannah as his home, one of the greatest handicaps that could be placed on him, he has shown that he is fit to fight and has passed over this and other hindrances that have been in his way until now he finds himself ready to fly away from the top of the ladder of collegiate attainment to make his way higher into the Halls of Justice.

name: William Thomas Harvey
motto: "You are not what you think you are, but what you think, you are."
enrolltime: During his two years at Georgia
clubs: end-man par excellence on the minstrel troupe of Georgia's Glee Club
description: "Happy" is a scholarly and accomplished gentleman. Modest and frank by nature, he is by turns as thoughtful as Socrates, and as merry as Bacchus. But at all times he is the life of the party with his engaging manners and irresistible wit. This latter characteristic has brought him into prominence as the end-man par excellence on the minstrel troupe of Georgia's Glee Club. During his two years at Georgia, spent in the study of Law, "Happy" has proved not only a diligent student, but also an athlete of no mean ability and a veritable stormy petrel at debate or at "bull sessions". A Past Master at repartee, his sparkling humor and ready wit make him at all times master of the situation. "Happy" is going to be a lawyer, and in his characteristic manner, will become a good one.

name: Louis Heiman, LL.B.
motto: "To live as gently as I can, To be, no matter where, a man."
description: Lou came to us from the Capital City, as one can ascertain by looking just under his picture. We have no doubt that in future years as he stands before a jury pleading his case, he will win it as he has won the admiration of his professors for his perseverance and consistency, and the love of his class-mates by his good nature, frankness, his care-free disposition, and friendliness toward everybody. Indeed, he is a warm friend, standing ready to help a pal in any possible way and to lend his sympathy when he cannot help. We know that Lou will carry to the legal profession the good nature and splendid ability that he has displayed in college. A true friend and a sterling gentleman,—that's Lou; and the ladies will add to this, we know. Our friendship will ever be with you—Lou. We predict a fine success for you in your work and real happiness in your life.

name: Joseph Cohen Hester, LL.B.
motto: "Amicos res optimae pariunt; adversae probant.—Prosperity begets friends; adversity proves them."
description: This stalwart son of Savannah came to the University from the Georgia Military Academy where he distinguished himself both as a student and a prince of good fellows. While at the University Joe has won many honors. He is a fine student, having delved deep into the magnitudinous intricacies of law with much success. In addition he is a social lion of repute, being one of the best and fanciest steppers in college. Joe has all the qualities a lawyer needs, to make a success, and we take pride in saying that he will make one of the best lawyers the University of Georgia ever graduated. Here's to you, Joe; may you and your magnetic personality and ready smile continue to be the dispeller of blues in the future as in the past at Georgia.

name: Emanuel Lewis, LL.B.
motto: "Do justice, love mercy, walk humbly with thy God."
clubs: President Henry W. Grady Speaking Club
description: In the fall of 1922 "Manny," of the state of Chatham, entered the law school; and now has accomplished the difficult task of graduating in two years. He not only completed all the work in this short period of time, but also graduated with the distinction of being one of the honor men of his class. His life and conduct here have been of such a nature that it must compel admiration. Quiet in nature, reserved in speech, and dignified in bearing, he has won more permanent friends than are usually allotted to a college career. With his natural endowments, a brilliant mind, the gift of expressive oratory, and a rare capacity for making friends, his future both in life and at the bar should be filled to the brim with success. "Manny," your friends are looking to you and are expecting you to accomplish the great things for which you are prepared.

name: William Chenault Munday, Jr., LL.B.
motto: "A good sense of humor is rather to be chosen than great riches."
description: William Chenault Munday, Jr.,—and there has never been another like him. For three years, he has been an indispensable factor in every phase of college life; as a student, delving into the mysteries of legal principles, and as an athlete and a newspaperman, putting his very soul into his work. Because of the regard in which he was held by his fellow students, Bill was elected President of the Junior Law Class and Editor-in-Chief of the Red and Black. Rich in humor, rare in wit, and lovable in nature, faithful to a trust, true to a cause, and loyal to a friend,—that's Bill; and may he spend his life amongst friends who are deserving of such as he.

name: Carlton K. Nelson, A.B., LL. B.
motto: "Knowing what to say, when, and how to say it, is man's greatest asset."
description: Carl's friends here are as numerous as his achievements. To know him better is to appreciate his friendship more. During his time here Carl has always fought for what he believed was right. Here's our best to you, "C. K." May you carry on as gallantly hereafter as you have heretofore.

name: Charles L. Padgett, LL.B.
motto: "Fight to the end—if perchance beaten, admit it, but try again."
clubs: Captain Cavalry R. O. T. C.
description: Ladies and Gentlemen, Behold—a student, orator, politician, teahound, and athlete, and an indomitable fighter withal. Very few people can claim the honor and distinction of possessing such a variety of arts, but "Padge" possesses all of these and more. For three years he has enjoyed a reputation as the outstanding lawyer of Doctor Sylvie's law school. He manifested his zeal and determination by passing the State Bar Examination after his first year of law, and by his work on the football field last year. He has succeeded in all of his undertakings, and is particularly noted in certain circles as a boxer. Loved by his friends and feared by his opponents, he has won much fame and many honors during his four years at college. We feel sure that one with his ambition and determination will never be defeated, and that he will become a lawyer that his Alma Mater will be proud of.

name: Burton Hugh Ramsey, LL.B.
motto: "Judge not your future by your past, but be determined, true and faithful. Let Honesty be your watchword, Truthfulness your daily companion."
description: "Pop" leaves us after three years, and during that time no one has been able definitely to determine his age. It is understood it ranges from 17 to 28, but the exact year is not known. Pop receives a degree in law and will retire to his home in Statesboro, where he will proceed to inoculate the judges with the overwhelming knowledge of law which Dr. Sylvanus Morris has pounded into his head. During his junior year in College, Ramsey made his spurs as a regular right fielder, playing a great brand of ball. He batted over .400, being one of the leading hitters in Southern College ball for the season. Pop is a fighter from the word go; judging by the grit and determination which he showed on the baseball field, and if he will apply the same kind of grit and determination to his legal profession he is sure to make a success.

name: Thomas Berrian Walton, Jr., LL.B.
motto: "To thine own self be true; and thou canst not then be false to any man."
enrolltime: one year in the School of Arts
clubs: Vice President and President Jeffersonian Moot Court
description: After coming to Georgia and spending one year in the School of Arts, Tom turned his attention to the study of the Law. Since that time his efforts have never failed to bear fruit, and throughout his whole college career he has been recognized as one of the most promising men in his class. Possessing that magnetic personality, which is so much to be desired by all men, he has made for himself a host of friends. True to his convictions, he has stamped himself as a diligent student, a loyal friend and a Man. Tom, our very best wishes will follow you throughout your life, and should the wheel of Fortune turn in the way which you so richly deserve, your life will be one of happiness and boundless success.

name: Ernest Prescott West, LL.B.
motto: "Be sure you are right, then go ahead."
description: From Wrightsville, Johnson County, Georgia, hails the Honorable "E. P." He has proven that he has the ability to play an important part for humanity's sake, and the old home town should feel proud of him just as his fellow-students of the Lumpkin Law School. Opportunity prevailed and he applied himself in accordance therewith. He has satisfied the requirements of the Law Department for three long years and is now ready for the great commencement of life's battles. West is a hard worker and a deep thinker, and has a knack of solving problems and a method of disposing of them in a sensible way. When the Law Department was called upon in 1923-24 to select a man to represent them on the Student Council "West" was the man for them. 'Nough said. He's a man amongst men. Speed on your way, "E. P." Let's have law and order. You have the best wishes of your many friends.

name: Fillmore Space, F.O.B.
motto: "Let every man occupy his niche; I will fill the rest."
description: Like his brother Phillup, this gentleman registered in college to make the 1924 Pandora possible. His singleness of purpose is only equalled by some of our athletic marvels. Although most of his friends agree that he's a peach of a fellow, a few have admitted that he was the apple of their eye, and some have dubbed him a lemon—but these ill-natured people are probably cynics. Both Phillup and Fillmore are generous boys whose minds are pure and simple, and whose lives are as an open book (blank book). It is these two boys who have become famous in the movies as the "Great open Spaces." Fillmore has one fault—he's a woman hater. This is explained by the fact that modern grils do not like to have much Space about their dates.
